The per-project brain is SINGLE-HOMED and git-committed at `<main-repo>/.vfkb` (DESIGN D2c). Commit `.vfkb/entries.jsonl` (append-only, merge=union — the SoR); the derived index (`index-meta.json`, `.sessions/`) is gitignored and rebuilt on read, never committed (ADR-0014).

Source-of-truth split: markdown ADRs in `docs/adr/` are the authoritative DECISION record (ADR-0001); the brain holds a `link` entry per ADR/RFC pointing at the repo-relative path and NEVER copies decision content (D1 constraint 4 / RFC-004). So there is no dual-SoR drift.
Build output `dist/` is gitignored; `npm test` runs `tsc` via pretest, so tests rebuild first. Never `docker build` expecting a committed `dist/` — rebuild before any image build.

Decision identity = nanoid (canonical, merge-safe); the human `ADR-NNNN` ordinal is stamped by the engine sole-writer at merge-to-main (ADR-0009). An RFC is a `decision` in `proposed` status (ADR-0007), promoted to an ADR on acceptance — no separate RFC entry type.

v1 retrieval bug: `query()` reused the INJECTION reranker for explicit SEARCH, discarding relevance — a held answer sorted to ~rank 90 was cut by `limit`, so the agent gave a confident WRONG answer. Fixed relevance-primary + light stemming (ADR-0016), distinct-term relevance floor (ADR-0017), cause-distinguished honest no-match (ADR-0018).

Failed approach: trusting the Phase-3 retrieval-quality gate — its fixtures were too small to expose scale ranking; a scale regression test now closes it.

dogfood-smoke check 6 (LLM driving MCP in one-shot `claude -p`) races the MCP cold-start and intermittently fails with "tools still connecting". It is NOT a regression: proven via a deterministic in-container JSON-RPC `tools/list` (all 7 tools incl `kb_search` advertised) plus `mcp.test.ts` green. Candidate follow-up: a wait-for-ready before the one-shot turn.

Search `score` is an UNNORMALIZED stemmed term-overlap count that includes repeats — a long entry hammering one common query token outscores a tight match on one DISTINCT term. The relevance floor uses distinct-term coverage (`matched / queryTermCount >= 1/3`), NOT raw score. Do not port AnythingLLM's 0.2 cosine value — only the mechanism transfers (its score is normalized cosine).

Evidence-gated build discipline: do not build speculatively. An RFC decides the SHAPE now; the BUILD triggers on observed evidence or an explicit operator request (e.g. RFC-003 embeddings — a 2nd live phrasing miss; ADR-0016 G1). "Runbook complete before execute."

Deterministic backstop > probabilistic gate: a structural/guardrail rule (e.g. Bash mutation tool-gating) is unit-tested deterministically (`gating.ts:isBrainWrite` + `guardrails.test.ts`), not left to the probabilistic L4 harness. A full paid L4 rerun is deferred as low-value when every behavior is unit-tested at HEAD.
Tier-B auto-capture SKIPS vfkb's own `kb_*` / `mcp__vfkb__*` tool calls (commit 31f4266) to avoid corpus self-pollution — the substrate must not record its own reads/writes as knowledge.

vfkb is developed in ~/VFKB/vfkb and tracks its OWN development by dogfooding this .vfkb brain (VFKB_DIR=.vfkb + the vfkb CLI) — NOT mykb / the kb workflow. See CLAUDE.md.

Status 2026-06-28: v1 (per-project tier) + H4 Track 1/4/4b all COMPLETE; in-repo H4 frontier EXHAUSTED (no in-order build remains). Rebranded vtfkb->vfkb (ADR-0026). 95/95 unit. Next work = a new fork -> re-ratify docs/H4-DEVELOPMENT-ROADMAP.md first.

Gated, do NOT build on spec: S1 embedding reranker (RFC-003, build on a 2nd live phrasing-robustness miss or explicit ask); P1 Claude-Code per-turn push (ADR-0015 Tier C, external-blocked upstream). Parked: H2 fleet wiring, H3 global tier.
Open finding: Claude Code's PostToolUse hook does NOT fire on a FAILED tool call -> live failure-capture is pi-only (external-block). live-capture-result scenario is harness-gated to pi.

Open finding: tool-gating (brain-write block) is FLAKY on the pi 0.73.1 substrate — holds only intermittently (guardrail integrity). A/B-confirmed NOT a code regression; needs its own investigation (re-ratify before fixing).
npm install here uses the corporate Nexus (nexus.optiscangroup.com) -> ENOTFOUND off-VPN. node_modules was bootstrapped by copying from ~/GitHub/vfkb. On VPN a normal install works.

Session handoff 2026-06-28: built a CODE knowledge-graph via the understand-anything skill -> .understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json (196 nodes / 410 edges, 8 layers, 11-step tour); view with /understand-dashboard, refresh with /understand (incremental via fingerprints.json). KEY FINDING: src/engine.ts is a ~700-line highest-fan-in hub (every face + curator/distiller/read import it) -> candidate refactor: split storage-writes / decision-family lifecycle / render+rerank / capture. Graph also CONFIRMS the clean 'one engine, two faces' layering (only inbound edges are test->src).

Open process gap (2026-06-28): nothing mechanically reminds the agent to kb_add — PostToolUse auto-capture is OFF here by design (deliberate knowledge only), and there is NO Stop hook. Proposed fix (NOT built): a Stop hook in .claude/settings.json that nudges record + resume-note before ending. Also unresolved: .understand-anything/ (graph artifacts) is NOT gitignored yet — decide commit-vs-ignore.
Pursue reliable decision-capture via a CONDITIONAL end-of-turn Stop-hook reminder (RFC-008), not a plain prose rule and not an every-turn nag.
RFC-008 — conditional end-of-turn Stop-hook decision-capture reminder (Proposed/WIP) docs/rfc/RFC-008-decision-capture-reminder.md

Stop-hook contract EMPIRICALLY VERIFIED at CLI v2.1.195 (host; both 'claude -p' print mode and stdin-piped non-print mode behave identically): (1) the Stop hook DOES fire at end-of-turn; (2) variant A {hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:Stop,decision:block,additionalContext:...}} WORKS — additionalContext reaches the agent and steers it (injected sentinel echoed in the reply); (3) a blocking Stop hook DOES loop without a guard (fire#1 block -> agent continues -> fire#2); (4) the harness DOES pass a NATIVE loop guard 'stop_hook_active' (false on first fire, true on re-entry) -> guard by checking that field, NO marker file needed. This FALSIFIES RFC-008's finding that v2.1.195 has no stop_hook_active guard. BONUS: Stop stdin is rich (cwd, session_id, transcript_path, last_assistant_message, effort) -> the 'decision unrecorded?' heuristic can read entries.jsonl + last_assistant_message. Re-verify if CLI version != 2.1.195.

Decouple RFC-008 (Stop-hook decision-capture reminder) from the L4-harness improvement work. RFC-008 proceeds on the verified Stop-hook contract toward accept->ADR; the L4 sandbox improvements (drift-warning banner, probe-mode beside L4 scenarios, readiness gate, cost/version telemetry) graduate to their OWN evidence-gated RFC (RFC-009) and are NOT prerequisites for RFC-008.
RFC-009 — L4 sandbox hardening + a home for Tier-0 platform-contract probes (Proposed/evidence-gated; split from RFC-008) docs/rfc/RFC-009-l4-harness-and-platform-probes.md

Accept RFC-008 -> ADR-0027: ship a conditional Stop-hook decision-capture reminder (vfkb hook stop). Native stop_hook_active loop guard; heuristic = uncommitted src/docs changes AND no new decision entry since HEAD; emits the verified decision:block + additionalContext contract; fail-open. Backed by deterministic unit tests + the Tier-0 probe as live evidence (NO L4 scenario - reasoned ADR-0023 exception). Wiring into .claude/settings.json is a SEPARATE operator-gated step.
ADR-0027 — conditional Stop-hook decision-capture reminder (accepts RFC-008; verified contract; native stop_hook_active guard) docs/adr/ADR-0027-stop-hook-decision-capture-reminder.md

Auto-layer wiring (.claude/settings.json, .mcp.json) MUST be validated in a sandbox before promotion to the live, dogfooded config. Process gap found: engine has unit tests, the platform contract has Tier-0 probes, agent-purpose has the L4 harness — but the wiring that ties them together was promoted straight to live (editing the tool while using it). Fix: a REPEATABLE scripted gate (scenarios/wiring-smoke.mjs) that drives REAL claude turns against the candidate settings.json in a throwaway sandbox and asserts the hook fires + the heuristic blocks/suppresses + the loop terminates; promote to live ONLY on green. Formalize in RFC-009 + an ADR.
ADR-0028 — auto-layer wiring is sandbox-validated before promotion to live config (scenarios/wiring-smoke.mjs) docs/adr/ADR-0028-sandbox-validate-auto-layer-wiring.md

Search/indexing crashed on a TAGLESS entry: index-store.ts did entry.tags.join(' ') assuming tags always present. Found by the decision-capture L4 scenario (a hand-written seed lacked tags; also a real risk for vfwb's lossy projection + legacy entries). Fixed at the READ BOUNDARY (storage.ts materialize() defaults tags:[]) + a defensive guard in index-store.ts; regression test in read.test.ts. NOTE: other read-path fields are still assumed-present (e.g. entry.validity.valid_until) — a fully malformed entry can still crash; that broader hardening is DEFERRED (see decision).

Scope the read-path robustness fix to the OBSERVED bug (tagless entries crashing search) only; DEFER guarding other assumed-present fields (validity, provenance, etc.) against arbitrarily-malformed entries as a separate, evidence-gated effort.

Sandbox isolation gotcha: symlinking dist/node_modules from a tmpdir sandbox INTO the repo lets a coding agent follow the symlink, infer the real project root, and 'helpfully' write src/ files into the REAL repo (observed: decision-capture smoke wrote src/config.ts into ~/VFKB/vfkb). Fix: use ABSOLUTE paths to repo dist/MCP in the sandbox (node resolves node_modules from the script location) so the sandbox holds NO breadcrumb back to the repo; add a leak-guard that removes known task files from the repo after each arm.

Add scenarios/decision-capture.mjs as the L4 purpose-demonstration for ADR-0027 (reminder-driven decision capture), REVISING ADR-0027's 'no L4 scenario' waiver. Contrast = Stop-hook ON vs OFF, same task, no 'record it' instruction; asserts a decision entry reflecting the choice lands in the sandbox .vfkb AND is retrievable (recall). Smoke (sonnet, N=1): vfkb 1/1 +recall, baseline 0/1 — DEMONSTRATED.
decision-capture L4 scenario (purpose proof for ADR-0027 reminder-driven capture; hook on/off contrast + recall) scenarios/decision-capture.mjs

Definition of Done (ADR-0029): a CAPABILITY (epic / feature / main group of tasks — NOT every change) is not 'done' until its real use-case is simulated end-to-end in a SANDBOX and OBSERVED to succeed — an agent-driven L4 scenario ('an e2e test driven by the agent to simulate the real use case'), DEMONSTRATED per ADR-0022, that MUST be able to fail (baseline/contrast/RED). Four clauses: isolated from live, observed-not-asserted, before-declaring-done, capable-of-failing. Generalizes ADR-0023 (was scoped to agent-observable) to any capability; sub-tasks/refactors/docs exempt (inner unit gates). Proof form fits the capability (agent-facing->L4; wiring->smoke-gate ADR-0028; external->Tier-0 probe; invariants->unit).
ADR-0029 — DoD: a capability is proven by an agent-driven sandboxed e2e use-case simulation (generalizes ADR-0023) docs/adr/ADR-0029-sandbox-proven-definition-of-done.md

Re-ratified the H4 roadmap (2026-06-29) to fold in the decision-capture fork as Track 6 COMPLETE: RFC-008->ADR-0027 (Stop-hook reminder, live), ADR-0028 (wiring smoke-gate), ADR-0029 (sandbox-proven DoD); decision-capture L4 DEMONSTRATED 3/3 vs 0/3 (config-format). In-repo H4 frontier EXHAUSTED again. Only open thread = confirm capture generalizes (error-strategy + id-scheme, sonnet N=3).

Decision-capture (ADR-0027 Stop-hook) GENERALIZES across tasks: re-ran scenarios/decision-capture.mjs (sonnet, N=3) on two further forced-decision tasks beyond the headline config-format. error-strategy: vfkb 3/3 +recall vs baseline 0/3 (agent chose Result<T> union). id-scheme: vfkb 3/3 vs baseline 0/3 (chose UUID v4, rejected nanoid for zero-dep). Capture is reminder-driven, not task-specific. NOTE: id-scheme showed capture-but-no-+recall purely because the scenario recall-probe searches expect[0]='id' (a 2-char token the stemmer filters as too short) — a probe-keyword weakness, NOT a capture/recall product gap; the entries are present. Records: scenarios/records/decision-capture.{error-strategy,id-scheme}.json.

Operator runbook: install & configure vfkb in a Claude Code CLI project (MCP server + 3 hooks + brain; install modes A self-host / B npm-link / C absolute / D npm-pkg unverified). Every step verified on Node v20.18.1 + CLI v2.1.195, 2026-06-29. docs/RUNBOOK-claude-code-integration.md

L4 record integrity: re-running a SINGLE scenario via l4-purpose.mjs rewrites the record's GLOBAL fields (generated timestamp, vfkb_sha, image_digest = docker inspect {{.Id}}) even though only that one scenario was re-validated. So a one-scenario refresh makes the committed multi-scenario record CLAIM the whole suite ran on the new image when 32/33 did not. Verified 2026-06-29: re-ran mcp-pull on pi (DEMONSTRATED 3/3) on the current vfkb-l4-pi:dev (Id sha256:5c37a85f, rebuilt 06-28 11:24); the record had been pinned to the now-gone d7a353b2. Mitigation: revert the record after a single-scenario validation (keep the coherent full-suite snapshot), or re-run the FULL suite to re-pin honestly. Candidate fix: store image_digest/timestamp PER-SCENARIO, not just globally.

Pi functionality fully re-validated (2026-06-29): full 33-scenario L4 suite at N=3 on the dockerized pi-coding agent (vfkb-l4-pi:dev, Id sha256:5c37a85f) = 32/33 DEMONSTRATED. The sole miss is tool-gating (0/3) — the KNOWN, A/B-confirmed-not-a-regression flaky guardrail on the pi 0.73.1 substrate (brain-write block holds only intermittently; log shows the HACKED{ write leaking past the gate). All MCP, continuity, distill, capture, and exclusion scenarios passed 3/3. This is the first coherent full-suite re-pin since the suite grew to 33 (was 22-23 historically). Record: scenarios/records/deepseek__deepseek-v4-pro__docker.{json,md}.

HANDOFF 2026-06-29 EOD. Done today: (1) re-ratified the H4 roadmap — folded the decision-capture fork in as Track 6 COMPLETE; in-repo H4 frontier EXHAUSTED again. (2) Confirmed decision-capture GENERALIZES: error-strategy 3/3+recall, id-scheme 3/3 vs baseline 0/3 (sonnet N=3). (3) Fixed id-scheme recall probe (search 'identifier' not stemmer-filtered 'id'). (4) Wrote docs/RUNBOOK-claude-code-integration.md (verified install/hook runbook). (5) Validated the dockerized pi-coding agent: full 33-scenario L4 suite N=3 = 32/33 DEMONSTRATED on vfkb-l4-pi:dev (Id 5c37a85f); coherent record re-pin. (6) Logged L4 record-integrity gotcha. NEXT (pick per roadmap, don't poll): the ONE genuine open thread is the tool-gating guardrail-integrity investigation — brain-write block leaks intermittently on the pi 0.73.1 substrate (0/3 this run; 'HACKED{' slips past the tool_call gate); re-ratify before fixing. Otherwise no roadmap-mandated build (S1/P1 gated, RFC-009/read-path-hardening evidence-gated/parked). Candidate cleanup: store L4 record image_digest/timestamp PER-SCENARIO (see gotcha df8ca4a5). A new build needs a fresh fork -> re-ratify the roadmap first.

CONSUMER-ONBOARDING evidence (2026-06-30): the vfwb maintainer/agent tried to migrate vfwb to consume vfkb and hit 5 concrete friction points — a real consumer fork (not self-hosting). FR-1 (P1) `vfkb init [project]`: idempotent scaffold of .mcp.json + .claude/settings.json (SessionStart/PreToolUse-gate/Stop, VFKB_PROJECT defaulted from dir name) + .gitignore stanza + empty .vfkb/entries.jsonl + a parameterized 'how we track work HERE' CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md snippet; prints the one approval step. FR-2 (P0, THE BLOCKER): portable engine resolution — kill the hard-coded path. Committed wiring currently uses relative `node dist/cli.js` / `dist/mcp-server.js` (VERIFIED 06-30: only resolves when cwd IS this engine repo; ~6 hardcoded VFKB_DIR/VFKB_PROJECT sites). Fix = single env indirection `node "$VFKB_HOME/vfkb.mjs"` + ship vfkb & vfkb-mcp as single-file esbuild bundles (MCP server must bundle @modelcontextprotocol/sdk + zod to be truly zero-dep). FR-3 (P1) `vfkb import` --from-mykb/--from-adr/--from-markdown (provenance=imported). FR-4 (P1) brain<->engine version stamp (schema_version+engine_commit in a .vfkb/manifest.json) + `vfkb doctor` (compat, wiring present, VFKB_PROJECT correct, MCP/hooks approved, warns on dual-clone committed-brain drift — a failure mode already tripped). FR-5 (P1 cheap) docs/CONSUMER-ONBOARDING.md + an ADR formalizing the consumer integration & distribution contract. This is a coherent NEW FORK ('vfkb as a consumable product') = standard-setting -> RFC -> ADR -> re-ratify roadmap (Track 7) BEFORE building. Dependency-forced order: ADR(framing) -> FR-2 -> FR-1 -> FR-4 -> FR-3 -> CONSUMER-ONBOARDING doc. DoD (ADR-0029): proof = sandbox a throwaway CONSUMER repo, set VFKB_HOME, run a real agent turn, observe SessionStart inject + PreToolUse gate blocks a direct .vfkb write + MCP kb_* resolve — extends scenarios/wiring-smoke.mjs (ADR-0028) self->consumer.
docs/rfc/RFC-010-consumer-integration-and-distribution.md

Branch + PR-first workflow (supersedes the old 'solo-dev: commit to main' rule). NEVER commit or push directly to main. Every change — code AND docs (RFCs/ADRs/CLAUDE.md) — lands on a topic branch and is delivered as a PR for the operator to review on GitHub; always report clickable URLs (PR + blob per changed file) after a push, unprompted. Definition of Done = the branch is MERGEABLE to main (green, review-ready), not 'committed to main'. Default interactive flow: open PR and stop for the operator to review/merge. The solo-dev self-merge latitude applies ONLY when the operator explicitly calls out autonomous mode, and even then stays branch->PR->merge (never a direct-to-main commit).

FR-2 bundle spike RESOLVED (2026-06-30, observed): esbuild 0.21.5 bundles src/mcp-server.ts into a SINGLE 1.1MB ESM file (vfkb-mcp.mjs, --bundle --platform=node --format=esm) with @modelcontextprotocol/sdk + zod inlined and ZERO externals. Run from a throwaway dir with NO node_modules (VFKB_DIR=tmp brain), it completed the MCP initialize handshake and tools/list advertised all 9 kb_* tools. So RFC-010 Open Item #1 is closed: the 'truly zero-dep single-file MCP server' is feasible — FR-2 does NOT need the vendored-bundle-dir fallback. Only the size warning (1.1MB) — cosmetic. cli.ts can bundle the same way for vfkb.mjs.

ACCEPTED RFC-010 -> ADR-0030: the consumer integration & distribution contract. vfkb becomes a consumable product for non-self repos via 5 FRs built in dependency-forced order: FR-2 portable engine ($VFKB_HOME indirection + single-file esbuild bundles vfkb.mjs/vfkb-mcp.mjs, kills the relative dist/ paths) -> FR-1 vfkb init (idempotent scaffold) -> FR-4 brain<->engine version stamp (.vfkb/manifest.json, committed) + vfkb doctor -> FR-3 vfkb import (mykb/adr/markdown, lossy) -> CONSUMER-ONBOARDING.md. The FR-2 single-file-bundle unknown was spiked & resolved BEFORE acceptance (fact 8c547ae0). DoD = scenarios/consumer-onboarding.mjs, an agent-driven sandbox scenario extending the ADR-0028 wiring smoke-gate self->consumer, must-fail contrast arm; live self-repo wiring migrates to $VFKB_HOME only AFTER the consumer sandbox proves it. Roadmap re-ratified with Track 7 (active frontier). Three defaults locked in the ADR: manifest.json committed (vs derived index-meta), lossy mykb import, $VFKB_HOME as a documented one-time per-machine step.
docs/adr/ADR-0030-consumer-integration-and-distribution.md

esbuild bundle (FR-2): do NOT add a `banner: { js: '#!/usr/bin/env node' }` when bundling src/cli.ts / src/mcp-server.ts. Both entry files already start with a shebang, which esbuild preserves at line 1; the banner emits a SECOND shebang on line 2 -> 'SyntaxError: Invalid or unexpected token' when node loads the .mjs. Fix: no banner — let esbuild hoist the source shebang. scripts/build-bundles.mjs builds dist/bundles/{vfkb.mjs (~48KB, zero-dep), vfkb-mcp.mjs (~1.1MB, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk+zod inlined)}; src/bundles.test.ts is the deterministic inner gate (runs both from a no-node_modules cwd: MCP tools/list=9, CLI add persists).

FR-1 `vfkb init [project]` shipped (src/init.ts + cli.ts dispatch + src/init.test.ts, 5 tests). Idempotently scaffolds a consumer repo: .vfkb/entries.jsonl (never clobbers an existing brain), .mcp.json (merges, keeps other servers), .claude/settings.json (3 hooks: SessionStart/PreToolUse-gate/Stop, merged per-event), .gitignore stanza (append-once), AGENTS.md snippet (append-once, marker <!-- vfkb:how-we-track-work -->); prints the one manual step (set $VFKB_HOME + approve MCP/hooks). WIRING-FORM CONTRACT (consumers depend on this): .mcp.json args use ${VFKB_HOME}/vfkb-mcp.mjs (Claude Code env-var EXPANSION in args), while .claude/settings.json hook commands use shell `node \"$VFKB_HOME/vfkb.mjs\" hook <sub>` (shell expansion). project name defaults to the cwd basename. 111/111 unit green.

Consumer-onboarding capability DEMONSTRATED 3/3 (2026-06-30, ADR-0030/0029 DoD). scenarios/consumer-onboarding.mjs drives REAL claude turns: PASS arm = a fresh repo onboarded via `vfkb init onboard-demo` (portable $VFKB_HOME bundles, NO node_modules in the sandbox) + one recorded fact -> a real claude turn answers the seeded sentinel ZEBRA-7731 (the SessionStart auto-layer delivered the repo's vfkb knowledge). CONTRAST/must-fail arm = a NOT-onboarded repo -> same turn replies UNKNOWN (trial 3: agent explored the empty dir, found nothing). 3/3 both arms. Exercises FR-1 (init) + FR-2 ($VFKB_HOME single-file bundle) end-to-end with a real agent. A deterministic pre-check (SessionStart injection must surface the seed) fails cheap before any model call. Proves vfkb is consumable by a non-self repo. NOTE: the live self-repo wiring migration to $VFKB_HOME is now UNBLOCKED (ADR-0028 sandbox-proven) but NOT yet done.

FR-4 shipped (brain<->engine version stamp + vfkb doctor; ADR-0030). src/version.ts: SCHEMA_VERSION=1 (the entries.jsonl envelope compat signal, ADR-0011) + ENGINE_VERSION/ENGINE_COMMIT. The decision `why` rationale is a NO-OP in the current engine. `vfkb add decision "..." --why "..."`: cli.ts cleanText() strips ALL `--flag value` pairs before addEntry, AddOpts has no `why`, KnowledgeEntry has no `why` field, and mcp-server.ts kb_add has no `why` param either. So --why / a why arg is silently DROPPED — decision rationale only persists if written into the entry TEXT. CLAUDE.md + ADR/RFC docs show `--why` as if it works (it doesn't). Impact: this session's decision captures that relied on why= would have lost rationale had the text not already been self-contained. Candidate fix (own decision/ADR): either add a real `why` field to the envelope + thread it through cli/mcp, or update the docs to fold rationale into text. Found while building FR-3 import (mykb decisions HAVE a why field -> folded into text as 'Why: ...').

All via addEntry role=import (Trust 'import') + 'imported' tag, provStatus preserved from mykb (verified) else unverified; malformed jsonl lines skipped not aborted. resolveMykbArea(name) falls back to ~/.mykb/areas/<name>. Real smoke: imported 181 entries from the real ~/.mykb 'mykb' area + 30 ADR links from docs/adr. 120/120 unit green.

NEXT-SESSION TASK (operator-requested 2026-06-30): close the two session-END continuity gaps so `/exit` is SAFE-BY-DEFAULT. GAP 1 — durable 'what's next' handoff has NO auto-capture: vfkb wiring has SessionStart/PreToolUse/Stop hooks but NO SessionEnd hook, and the Stop hook only REMINDS about decisions (ADR-0027), it doesn't write a handoff. So the 'next session, pick up X' pointer is hand-recorded as a fact every time. GAP 2 — nothing auto-commits the brain (it ships INSIDE the repo, ADR-0019); `/exit` leaves new entries uncommitted -> invisible to a fresh clone / next session. CANDIDATE FIX: a SessionEnd hook that (a) auto-records (or reliably prompts for) a handoff entry and (b) stages/commits the brain. STARTING POINTS / CONTEXT: (i) first EMPIRICALLY VERIFY the Claude Code SessionEnd hook contract (does it exist on this CLI version, what stdin/stdout, can it run commands) the same way the Stop-hook contract was verified (gotcha d70c0299 / RFC-008). (ii) DESIGN TENSION: the branch+PR-first workflow (decision 34f2f2da — NEVER commit to main) means any auto-commit must target the CURRENT working branch or open a PR, NOT main — reconcile this in the RFC. (iii) decisions-before-code -> write an RFC first (candidate 'Track 8: session-end continuity Brake'); the ADR-0021 lesson (a prose rule with no Brake gets ignored) argues for a real mechanism over prose, mirroring the RFC-008->ADR-0027 decision-capture build. (iv) note: the resume DIGEST is already auto-derived (ADR-0020), so only the handoff-write + commit are missing.

SessionEnd hook contract EMPIRICALLY VERIFIED (CLI v2.1.196) + cross-checked vs official docs (code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks.md). FINDINGS: (1) SessionEnd DOES fire (observed on `claude -p` completion); per docs it also fires on /clear, logout, resume, crash, Ctrl-C — it CANNOT block/delay exit (fire-and-forget, exit code IGNORED). (2) It CAN run arbitrary shell commands (10-min timeout per docs); cwd = the project dir (observed PWD=sandbox), so `git add .vfkb && git commit` targets the right repo — GAP-2 auto-commit is FEASIBLE. (3) stdin JSON fields: session_id, transcript_path, cwd, prompt_id, hook_event_name, reason. NOTE: NO last_assistant_message (unlike the Stop hook) — to derive a handoff the hook must READ transcript_path (.jsonl). (4) `reason` enum (docs): clear|resume|logout|prompt_input_exit|bypass_permissions_disabled|other. DISCREPANCY: docs say `-p` => prompt_input_exit, but OBSERVED `claude -p "arg"` => reason:"other" (prompt_input_exit is for PIPED stdin EOF, not an arg prompt). So a reason-matcher is brittle — prefer running on ALL reasons (or guard inside the hook). (5) stdout IS schema-validated: emitting {hookSpecificOutput:{additionalContext}} (the Stop-hook shape) FAILS validation with a visible (non-fatal, exit 0) warning. additionalContext does NOTHING on SessionEnd (session is ending — CANNOT inject into next session). Allowed output fields: continue, suppressOutput, stopReason, decision(approve|block), reason, systemMessage, terminalSequence, permissionDecision. => the impl should emit NOTHING (silent run = no warning, confirmed). DESIGN IMPLICATION for Track 8: SessionEnd can AUTO-COMMIT the brain (GAP 2) but CANNOT prompt the agent for a handoff (GAP 1) — the prompting must stay on the Stop hook (which CAN inject); SessionEnd only commits / optionally auto-derives a handoff from the transcript. Re-verify if CLI version != 2.1.196.

Drafted RFC-011 (Proposed): session-end continuity — safe-by-default `/exit`. Splits the two end-of-session gaps by the empirically-verified SessionEnd contract (gotcha f0e913b97824): GAP 2 (uncommitted brain) -> a new SessionEnd hook `vfkb hook session-end` that auto-commits ONLY `.vfkb/entries.jsonl`, NEVER on main (branch guard honors decision 34f2f2da), scoped (never `git add -A`), attribution-free message, no push (operator owns the PR); GAP 1 (no handoff) -> a Stop-hook handoff reminder reusing the ADR-0027 pattern (SessionEnd CANNOT inject/prompt — additionalContext is a no-op at end of session — so the nudge must stay on the Stop hook), nudging the agent to write a COMMITTED `fact` tagged handoff/next (NOT a resume-note, which is gitignored/local-only per ADR-0019). WHY: the brain ships inside the repo (ADR-0019) and cross-clone continuity lives in committed entries, so `/exit` leaving entries uncommitted silently breaks continuity; "remember to commit/handoff" is a prose rule and ADR-0021 says prose rules get ignored -> needs a mechanism. DoD (ADR-0029): GAP 2 -> deterministic smoke-gate (commit on branch / NOT on main / scoped / no-op when clean); GAP 1 -> agent-driven L4 (handoff entry present+recallable vs Stop-hook-OFF contrast). NEXT: operator review of the RFC PR -> accept->ADR (Track 8, re-ratify roadmap) + wire .claude/settings.json after smoke-gate green, or withdraw.
docs/rfc/RFC-011-session-end-continuity.md

Self-review of RFC-011 (PR #17) -> 2 substantive design corrections + 2 scope caveats (amended in place). (1) GAP-2 commit-scope BUG: the first draft's `git add .vfkb/entries.jsonl && git commit` would sweep in ANY files the operator had already STAGED for their own commit -> must be a pathspec/only commit `git commit -o -- .vfkb/entries.jsonl -m ...` (stages just that path for this commit, leaves the rest of the index untouched). (2) GAP-1 timing mismatch (the central open question): the Stop hook fires EVERY TURN but a handoff is an END-OF-SESSION artifact, so a Stop-only nudge nags mid-session. REVISED recommendation (was: Stop nudge primary, auto-derive parked): lead with B2 (SessionEnd auto-DERIVES a fallback handoff from transcript_path at true session-end = correct timing, lower quality) as the reliable FLOOR, add B1 (Stop nudge, agent-authored = higher quality) only as a gated once-per-session strong-signal nudge; L4 contrasts B1/B2/B1+B2 to settle empirically. GAP 2 is the real safety net and ships first regardless. Scope caveats added: SessionEnd verified under `claude -p` only (interactive /exit inferred); arbitrary-execution+cwd observed but the in-hook `git commit` and `systemMessage` are inferred -> confirm in the smoke-gate. WHY: a bare commit silently corrupting the operator's staged set, and a per-turn handoff nag, are exactly the kind of mechanism flaws ADR-0028 (sandbox-validate wiring) exists to catch before live wiring.

GAP-1 design constraint (verified 2026-06-30): KB_SESSION_ID is NOT set in this repo's live hook wiring (.claude/settings.json hookCommand is just `VFKB_DATA_DIR=.vfkb VFKB_PROJECT=vfkb node ...bootstrap... cli hook <sub>` — no KB_SESSION_ID; bootstrap doesn't set it either). SessionState.load() returns an EPHEMERAL in-memory state when KB_SESSION_ID is unset (file=null) -> `vfkb resume-note` does NOT persist, and NO per-session record is written during normal hook operation. The two records in .vfkb/.sessions/ (a UUID + a hand-named graph-session) were created when an id WAS set / by hand. IMPLICATIONS: (1) the cleanest B2 GAP-1 variant — "promote the session resume-note to a committed handoff entry" — is NON-VIABLE as wired (no persisted note to promote). (2) Any session-record-based GAP-1 mechanism is unreliable here. (3) The ONLY robust state-free signal at hook time is the git-HEAD-delta of entries.jsonl (append-only, ADR-0019) — exactly what stop-reminder.ts already uses (uncommittedDecisionCount/hasUncommittedWork, no KB_SESSION_ID). So a viable GAP-1 must be git-delta-based: B1 (Stop nudge: fire when uncommitted work AND no uncommitted handoff/next fact since HEAD) or B2 (SessionEnd: if no handoff/next entry among new-since-HEAD, write a minimal auto handoff enumerating the session's new entries). (4) SEPARATE QUESTION worth confirming: whether the SessionStart resume digest (ADR-0020) is actually exercising session records here at all, given KB_SESSION_ID is unset — the digest may be running off an empty/ephemeral session + the brain only.

BUILT Track 8 GAP 1 = the B2 deterministic handoff floor (operator-chosen 2026-06-30 over a metered B1/B2 L4 contrast; folded into ADR-0033). At SessionEnd, runSessionEnd (src/session-end.ts) computes the entries appended since HEAD (newEntriesSinceHead — git-HEAD-delta, the same state-free signal stop-reminder.ts uses); if NONE is tagged handoff/next, it writes a fallback `fact` (writeAutoHandoff: role executor, zone established, tags handoff,next,auto) ENUMERATING the session's new entries (id/type/snippet, cap 12), BEFORE the pathspec commit so it ships in the same commit. If the agent already authored a handoff, B2 stays out. WHY git-delta not resume-note: KB_SESSION_ID is unset in the live wiring (gotcha e8f324dc) -> SessionState/resume-note don't persist, so the resume-note-promotion variant was non-viable. WHY deterministic floor not the metered L4: deterministic backstop > probabilistic gate; guarantees a committed forward pointer in a fresh clone (surfaces via the knowledge bundle, which reads committed entries, independent of session records) with no LLM spend. Gate: src/session-end.test.ts now 8 cases (added: writes+commits auto-handoff when none recorded; stays out when one exists). Bundle smoke confirmed: 2 knowledge entries + 1 committed [handoff,next,auto] entry, brain clean after. 137/137 unit green. OPEN/evidence-gated: B1 higher-quality agent-authored Stop nudge + the metered L4 quality contrast (B2 vs B1+B2) — build only on request or evidence the floor is insufficient.

Fixed issue #22 (ADR-0035): vfkb init now emits the four Claude Code hooks anchored to ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-.} instead of bare CWD-relative paths. Hooks run in the SESSION cwd (follows cd), not the repo root, so 'node .vfkb/bin/bootstrap.mjs' threw MODULE_NOT_FOUND once a session cd'd out of root — silently disabling the PreToolUse write-gate + SessionEnd auto-commit; affected every onboarded repo incl. vfkb itself. src/init.ts hookCommand() anchors VFKB_DATA_DIR + the bootstrap path; the settings merge now UPGRADES an existing relative vfkb hook in place (keeps user hooks) so re-running init repairs onboarded repos; doctor gains a 'hooks anchor' WARN. Scope = hooks only (the .mcp.json server spawns ONCE at cwd=root, not re-invoked). vfkb's own .claude/settings.json regenerated to the anchored form. DoD (ADR-0029): a deterministic init.test gate runs the emitted hook from a FOREIGN cwd (resolves->INACTIVE payload, exit 0) vs the old bare form (MODULE_NOT_FOUND) — capable of failing; 155/155 green; live-confirmed. Why ${VAR:-.} not bare ${VAR}: an unset var expands to /.vfkb (hard fail, worse than relative) — the :-. floors at today's behavior.

Claude Code hooks emitted by 'vfkb init' used CWD-relative paths and BREAK when the session cwd leaves the repo root (MODULE_NOT_FOUND on .vfkb/bin/bootstrap.mjs). EMPIRICALLY VERIFIED at CLI v2.1.197 (sandbox probe + official docs): (1) $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR IS injected into every hook command's env = absolute project root, and is CWD-INDEPENDENT — a hook that cd's to /tmp still sees $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=repo-root while $PWD=/tmp; (2) hook 'command' strings run via 'sh -c' so ${VAR:-default} parameter-expansion resolves; (3) hook cwd = the SESSION cwd (follows cd), NOT guaranteed to be repo root. Fix = anchor to ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-.} (ADR-0035, fix/issue-22). NOTE the MCP server in .mcp.json is NOT affected (spawned once at session start, cwd=root, not re-invoked). Re-verify if CLI != 2.1.197.

RFC-012 (Track 9 Q1, proposed): deterministic contradiction surfacing at write time. On every explicit kb_add/add, run a deterministic lexical conflict check (findConflictCandidates: live entries sharing type-or-tag with distinct-stemmed-term overlap over threshold, extending the ADR-0021 findLexicalDuplicates seam) and append candidate conflicts to the ADD RESULT — the write always lands first (D3d unchanged), never blocks, never auto-supersedes, no LLM on the write path (TOKI). Passive capture exempt. Plus a propose-only 'curate conflicts' sweep. DoD: L4 contradiction-surface (seeded stale value + corrected info in-task; vfkb arm ends with one live truth vs contrast arm with contradictory duplicates; RED first, both harnesses) + unit fixtures for the detector.

Why: arXiv:2606.27472 shows agents do not supersede stale memories unprompted and more budget does not help; vfkb lived it — gotcha 91338268 stayed live-and-wrong for 2 days after its fix landed, caught only by an unrelated inventory. Contradictions are near-misses, so the exact-match curate-time dup check can never catch them; write time is when the fresh info is in-context.

Discussion notes — multi-agent dev-concurrency corner cases (not a decision, no RFC yet): docs/NOTES-multi-agent-concurrency-corner-cases.md. Catalogs corner cases for multiple dev agents (e.g. concurrent Claude Code sessions) building one project concurrently — shared-clone file/git-lock races, separate-branch git-merge races on the brain and on prose docs, GitHub self-merge races, infra races — grounded in vilonotes' own dogfooding of vfkb. Includes an empirically verified fact: two branches independently appending to the tail of a JSONL file from a common ancestor DO produce a git merge conflict (tested live, not assumed). Also works through whether hosting the brain as a live service (agents access only via MCP, no local git-committed file) would fix these — it cleanly fixes the entries.jsonl merge-conflict and TOCTOU/stale-visibility cases, leaves all code-level git races untouched, and introduces new problems (single point of failure, needs real auth, loses git's review-gate, and reverses ADR-0019's "brain ships with the repo" principle — would need its own ADR amendment, not a quiet swap).

vfkb v2 vision doc (proposed/pre-RFC consolidation, not decided): docs/V2-VISION.md. Consolidates the 2026-07-05 brainstorm into initiatives: concurrency/merge safety (git merge driver for entries.jsonl tail-append conflicts, advisory lock for the TOCTOU gap, per-entry session_id/agent_id attribution), schema honesty (real `why` field, full envelope validation at the read boundary, formalized contradiction/supersede fields), scale (rebuildable index instead of readAll()'s full-file rescan per call), a pluggable storage-backend interface (JSONL stays the ADR-0019 default; hosted is opt-in, not a reversal), and known warts (GAP-1, per-scenario L4 record digests, a concurrent-writer test in the pyramid, RFC-003 revisit reasoning). Central correction+insight in §1: the "reuse mykb's multi-project mechanism for multi-session" framing was subtly wrong — verified against IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md's Lessons-from-mykb record and session.ts: L4 (one global `.active` pointer -> concurrent sessions silently clobber) was ALREADY specifically the multi-session fix (KB_SESSION_ID-keyed `.sessions/<id>.json`, day one), a separate axis from mykb's single-brain-for-many-projects concern (settled differently via per-project brain, D2e/D2g; VFKB_PROJECT is confirmed just a doctor-consistency label, not a scope key). The mechanism already has the right shape (partition-by-id, never a shared mutable singleton) and already exposes SessionState.records() as a de facto concurrent-session registry — it just isn't engaged: verified none of this repo's 4 live hook commands in .claude/settings.json set KB_SESSION_ID, so SessionState.load() is always ephemeral today (matches the existing GAP-1 finding). Concrete cheap fix specified: Claude Code hook stdin already carries a real session_id per invocation (verified earlier for Stop/SessionEnd payloads) -> cli hook <sub> should read it from stdin instead of depending on an env var nothing sets. That closes GAP-1 outright and is the foundation several other v2 initiatives build on.

V2-VISION.md's §1 fix (read session_id from Claude Code hook stdin) was stress-tested against a second, very different real harness — vilonotes' kagent-hosted researcher — by reading ~/VFKB/vilonotes/researcher/run.py and backend/app.py directly (not assumed). RESULT: the fix generalizes cleanly (vilonotes' own .claude/settings.json wires the same 4 hooks, none set KB_SESSION_ID either, so GAP-1 independently reproduces there and the stdin-based fix needs zero changes to run.py). But the deep-dive surfaced a NEW, more serious, previously-unrecorded gap distinct from anything else in the doc: in-pod `kb_add` writes are NOT durably reconciled. The researcher pod calls kb_add live against its own local .vfkb/entries.jsonl (mcp__vfkb is in its ALLOWED_TOOLS), but the durable write path (backend/app.py's _land_commit) runs in a totally separate throwaway git worktree off origin/main and SYNTHESIZES ITS OWN single fact string ("SESSION HANDOFF..."/"ARTIFACT COMMITTED...") — it never reads or merges the pod's actual local entries.jsonl content. Compounding this, refresh_clone()'s own docstring admits prior-session kb_add writes are exactly what the NEXT new session's `git reset --hard origin/<branch>` would destroy (refresh is throttled to once-per-new-session specifically to bound, not eliminate, this loss window). Net effect: anything the researcher records live via kb_add beyond the one curated fact the backend later synthesizes is scratch state — survives at most until the next session starts on that pod, never reaches main. Also found: vilonotes already has an uncommitted `_turn_lock = asyncio.Lock()` (on branch feat/parallel-research-sessions) serializing ALL turn execution across ALL sessions in the pod — a coarse, hand-rolled, whole-turn-granularity workaround built at the orchestration layer because vfkb itself offers no finer-grained lock primitive; this is the concrete argument for vfkb owning its own lock scoped to just the read-decide-append critical section. Full writeup in docs/V2-VISION.md §2.

vfkb v2 development uses a dedicated long-lived `v2` branch; `main` stays release-only and never receives v2 code. Every v2 initiative from docs/V2-VISION.md branches from `v2` and PRs back into `v2`, never into `main`. `main` keeps receiving v1 hotfixes as before; `main` is merged into `v2` regularly (after every hotfix, not batched) to avoid divergence. Exception: docs (RFCs/ADRs/vision notes, incl. V2-VISION.md itself) are non-breaking and keep landing on `main` via the normal PR flow. `v2` gets the same branch protection as `main` (no direct pushes, PR required). Cutover (merge v2 into main, or promote v2 to be the new main) is a future decision, not this one. Full record: ADR-0036. Why: v2 is explicitly breaking-changes-allowed (the roadmap re-ratification CLAUDE.md's "Current state" already flagged as the next fork), but main is the released version consumers (incl. vilonotes via kagent) depend on staying stable — developing v2 directly on main would force freezing v1 fixes or leaking half-built breaking changes into the release. This is the standard pattern for a breaking next-major developed alongside a still-supported release.
ADR-0036 — v2 two-branch strategy: docs/adr/ADR-0036-v2-two-branch-strategy.md

ADR-0036 executed: PR #31 (V2-VISION.md) and PR #32 (ADR-0036 itself) both merged to main. The `v2` branch was created off main and pushed. Branch protection was added to BOTH `main` and `v2` via the GitHub API (previously `main` had ZERO GitHub-enforced protection — verified 404 on the branches/main/protection endpoint before this — enforcement had been convention-only via CLAUDE.md discipline). Applied: enforce_admins=true, required_pull_request_reviews.required_approving_review_count=0 (solo-dev, no-reviewers-required latitude), allow_force_pushes=false, allow_deletions=false, no required status checks, no push restrictions. Verified live: a direct empty-commit push to main was rejected by GitHub ("GH006: Protected branch update failed... Changes must be made through a pull request"), confirming the protection is real, not just documented. v2 development can now begin: initiatives branch from `v2`, PR into `v2`; docs keep landing on `main`.

v2 RFC batch (all Proposed, pre-acceptance): RFC-014 docs/rfc/RFC-014-session-backbone.md (real session_id from hook stdin, widened SessionData, entry attribution — foundational, everything else depends on it), RFC-015 docs/rfc/RFC-015-native-concurrency-lock.md (vfkb-owned lock scoped to read-decide-append, depends on RFC-014), RFC-016 docs/rfc/RFC-016-entries-jsonl-merge-driver.md (git merge driver for the verified tail-append conflict), RFC-017 docs/rfc/RFC-017-schema-honesty.md (real why field, envelope validation, contradiction/supersede fields), RFC-018 docs/rfc/RFC-018-rebuildable-index.md (rebuildable index replacing readAll()'s linear scan), RFC-019 docs/rfc/RFC-019-storage-backend-abstraction.md (pluggable storage interface, JSONL stays default). These are the first slice-up of docs/V2-VISION.md's initiatives, per ADR-0036's process (RFC first, ADR on acceptance — confirmed with the operator rather than assumed, since this repo's own house style treats technical/architectural designs differently from pure process decisions like ADR-0036 itself). Each will build on the `v2` branch only once accepted.

RFC-014..019 revised after an independent adversarial review (fable model, no prior session context, verified every claim against source/git history/a live merge-conflict reproduction). Fixes applied: (1) RFC-017's headline claim was FALSE — `--why` was already fixed 2026-06-30 commit 5ff56fc, five days before the RFC was drafted from a stale gotcha memory (91338268) without re-checking current code; corrected to the narrower, true gap (why persists as folded text, but has no structural field) and acknowledged directly, not defended. (2) RFC-015's DoD was unfalsifiable — synchronous in-process N-writers can't race on one Node event loop; fixed to require real child processes or an injected pause, plus a must-fail arm, plus cited ADR-0013 against the `flock` example (needs a native binding). (3) RFC-016 dismissed `merge=union` without engaging storage.ts's own "Order-independent in `updated` -> merge=union safe" comment (materialize() already tolerates arbitrary order/dedup, so the custom driver's real value is much narrower than claimed); revised to propose merge=union as v1, custom driver as a possible later refinement, and added that custom merge drivers can't run on GitHub's server-side PR merge (needs local git config never seen by GitHub) — added an explicit unverified-flag and a required empirical check (open a real conflicting test PR) to the DoD. (4) RFC-018 had the weakest evidence in the batch (114 entries, no observed slowness) and an unexamined tension (ADR-0014's content-hash check itself requires a full parse, undercutting the index's point); revised to ratify-shape-but-gate-build like RFC-003, and to prefer incremental append-offset parsing over content-hash reuse. (5) RFC-019's "95/95" was stale (actual 155/155) — corrected. (6) RFC-014 strengthened: cited that session-end's hook already parses session_id from stdin today (src/cli.ts, just doesn't persist it) as existing-pattern precedent, and promoted the --resume session-id-stability assumption from a build-time nice-to-check to an acceptance precondition. All six still Proposed pending operator review; none self-merged.

VERIFIED 2026-07-06 (CLI v2.1.201): Claude Code hook-stdin session_id IS STABLE across `claude -p --resume`. Probe: sandbox with a Stop hook appending its stdin JSON to a log; run 1 `claude -p` produced session_id 841fd5c2-... (both in the -p JSON result and the hook payload); run 2 `claude -p --resume 841fd5c2-...` delivered the SAME session_id to the hook. So per-session state keyed on hook-stdin session_id (RFC-014/ADR-0039) accumulates correctly across resumed turns of one conversation — the acceptance precondition RFC-014 required holds. Caveats: single-machine, non-interactive -p probe; interactive-session resume and /resume were not separately probed; re-verify on a CLI major change (contract verified at 2.1.196/2.1.197 has held through 2.1.201).

Acceptance ADR batch 2026-07-06 (ordinals 0037–0044): ADR-0037 docs/adr/ADR-0037-contradiction-surfacing-at-write.md (← RFC-012), ADR-0038 docs/adr/ADR-0038-cross-project-brain-query.md (← RFC-013), ADR-0039 docs/adr/ADR-0039-session-backbone.md (← RFC-014, precondition verified), ADR-0040 docs/adr/ADR-0040-native-concurrency-lock.md (← RFC-015), ADR-0041 docs/adr/ADR-0041-entries-jsonl-merge-union.md (← RFC-016 as revised), ADR-0042 docs/adr/ADR-0042-schema-honesty.md (← RFC-017 as corrected), ADR-0043 docs/adr/ADR-0043-rebuildable-index-shape.md (← RFC-018, build gated), ADR-0044 docs/adr/ADR-0044-storage-backend-abstraction.md (← RFC-019).

AUDIT 2026-07-06 — how the ADR-0037..0044 batch addresses multi-agent-on-branches (mapped against all 15 corner cases in docs/NOTES-multi-agent-concurrency-corner-cases.md). SEPARATE-BRANCHES topology: no live race exists while agents work (each clone has its own entries.jsonl); the collision funnels into merge time, where ADR-0041 merge=union kills the guaranteed conflict (#8) and most careless-resolution loss (#9) — safe because materialize() is order-independent/dedup-by-newest-updated; ADR-0039 session_id stamping gives post-merge attribution at the knowledge layer (#13's brain half); ADR-0037's write-time check + `vfkb curate conflicts` sweep catch semantic contradictions that survive a clean textual merge; ADR-0042 read-boundary validation protects against entries arriving via others' merges. SHARED-DIR topology: ADR-0040 lock closes TOCTOU (#7) — scoped to one VFKB_DATA_DIR by design (across clones there is no shared file; that is 0041's job). UNTOUCHED (correctly out of scope): #1-5 (file stomping, index.lock, silent SessionEnd failure, branch-switch, ports), #10 (prose-doc conflicts — union unsafe for non-append shapes), #11/#14/#15. THREE AUDIT FLAGS for future sessions: (1) ADR-0041's fix is UNVERIFIED on the repo's actual merge path — whether GitHub's server-side merge honors merge=union is an open empirical check, part of the build DoD; if negative, branch-agents merging via PR still conflict (trivial to resolve but stops automated flows — the actual pain). (2) Corner case #12 (live cross-branch visibility — agents making contradicting calls for hours with no signal until merge) is the KNOWN residual gap no accepted RFC covers; the NOTES doc said so, RFC-015 took only the same-dir half; ADR-0044's storage seam is the deliberate door to the hosted-backend fix, not built now. (3) ADR-0037 is write/sweep-time, not merge-time — nothing auto-runs the detector when a merge lands; a post-merge `curate conflicts` step (CI/hook) is the obvious small follow-up, worth a line in whatever future RFC takes the #12 slot.

V2-1 session backbone (ADR-0039) BUILT 2026-07-06 on branch feat/v2-session-backbone (from v2). Scope shipped: effectiveSessionId() in src/session.ts (KB_SESSION_ID overrides the harness stdin id); all four Claude Code hooks parse session_id from their own stdin and thread it into SessionState.load() — SessionStart persists the record, Stop bumps turnCount on the non-re-entry path (stop_hook_active guard prevents double-count), PostToolUse stamps captures + records capturedIds into the right session, SessionEnd threads the id into runSessionEnd + the B2 auto-handoff entry; SessionData widened with agentRole/agentLabel (from VFKB_AGENT_ROLE/LABEL env when set), branch (best-effort git symbolic-ref at record creation), pid; KnowledgeEntry gains optional session_id, stamped by addEntry from opts.sessionId ?? KB_SESSION_ID (so CLI/MCP writes stamp only when env is set — the MCP server has no per-call harness id; honest limitation); SessionState.records() documented as the concurrent-session registry (ADR-0040's lock will log against it). PROOF (ADR-0029, observed): unit 167/167 (10 new in test/session-backbone.test.ts, run RED first — 8/10 failed pre-fix); L4 scenarios/session-backbone.mjs (host-sandboxed, real claude -p haiku, absolute-path hooks, KB_SESSION_ID unset): FIXED arm 11/11 (two sessions + one --resume turn → 2 isolated records, s1 turnCount==2 across resume, captures stamped s1/s2 correctly, capturedIds in the right records); BASELINE arm vs pre-fix origin/v2 dist: no .sessions dir, no stamps — the scenario can fail. v2 review gate created 2026-07-06 (operator-requested "after every major implementation launch a review agent"): the project command .claude/commands/v2-review.md — an adversarial, fresh-eyes subagent review run on every v2 initiative branch BEFORE its merge into v2. Rubric: (1) ground truth from the governing ADR + full diff, never the PR description; (2) ADR conformance — scope creep and silent scope drops both flagged; (3) the proof can fail (ADR-0029) — would the test pass with the feature deleted?; (4) repo protocols — fail-open hooks, deterministic backstop, append-only/materialize() semantics, no secrets, no AI attribution; (5) concrete-failure-scenario correctness hunt; (6) ranked findings + MERGE/FIX-FIRST/REDESIGN verdict. Wired into V2-ROADMAP's update protocol: an initiative is not DONE until its gate has run; outcomes recorded in the brain (tags v2,review) and noted in the merge PR. The harness's built-in /code-review exists but is generic — this command encodes what vfkb specifically cares about.

Why: A generic diff review misses the failure modes this repo has actually hit (proofs that cannot fail, stale-memory claims, scope drift from the accepted ADR). Encoding the rubric as a committed command makes the gate durable across sessions and operators, per the house rule that standing process lives in repo docs, not chat history.

V2-2 REVIEW GATE (first run of the /v2-review protocol, 2026-07-06, adversarial subagent on feat/v2-concurrency-lock): verdict FIX-FIRST, all findings fixed before merge. F1 HIGH (demonstrated, not theoretical): release unlinked the lockfile WITHOUT ownership check — a holder whose critical section outlives STALE_MS(10s) gets broken by a waiter, then its finally unlinks the WAITER's fresh lock, admitting an unbounded chain of overlapping writers (reviewer empirically drove a third process into a live critical section). Fix: unique per-acquisition token in the holder file; release unlinks only if the content is still ours. Regression test added (foreign lock planted mid-hold survives the holder's release). F2: pidAlive() treated EPERM as dead — an alive holder owned by another user (shared brain dir) got its lock broken instantly; fix: EPERM = alive. F3: the stale-lock test could NOT fail (the 5s fail-open also yields OK) — fixed with an elapsed<4s assertion proving the break, not the timeout. F4: .vfkb/.lock added to .gitignore (a crash-leaked lock would have been hand-committed via 'git add .vfkb'). F5: race-test barrier/hold widened (800ms/500ms) against CI spawn stagger. Reviewer also VERIFIED the proof-can-fail discipline by deleting the lock from dist and observing 3 winners. LESSON: the gate caught a real mutual-exclusion violation that 172 green tests + a passing DoD missed — the reviewer's empirical instrumentation (neutralize the feature, drive the pathological timeline) is the load-bearing part of the rubric, keep it.

RFC-016/ADR-0041 open item SETTLED empirically 2026-07-06 (throwaway private repo vilosource/vfkb-union-probe, created+deleted same session): (1) GitHub's server-side PR merge does NOT honor `merge=union` — with .gitattributes committed in the repo, the guaranteed-conflict PR (B appends after A merged) computed CONFLICTING DIRTY. (2) The workaround is now one command with zero manual resolution: a LOCAL `git merge` on the PR branch fires the union driver automatically (both entries present, no conflict markers, observed), and pushing that flips GitHub to MERGEABLE — the merge button then works (observed end-to-end). DISPOSITION (the follow-up decision ADR-0041 deferred): accept the GitHub-side gap; do NOT adopt a local-merge-and-push-to-main workflow (keeps the PR audit trail). Codified practice: when a PR shows CONFLICTING on .vfkb/entries.jsonl, run `git merge <base>` on the PR branch locally (union resolves it) and push — replacing the manual union+dedupe procedure used 6+ times in this session. Engine safety net unchanged: materialize() dedups by id/newest-updated, so even a duplicate-carrying union result reads correctly.

Why: The ADR flagged GitHub-honoring as unverified and required the empirical check before claiming the problem solved end-to-end. The probe answered it: negative on the button, but the attribute converts the failure mode from silent-data-loss-prone hand resolution to an automatic, lossless one-command step — the accept-the-gap option with most of the pain gone. A local-merge-to-main workflow would sacrifice the PR review/audit trail for marginal gain.

V2-4 review gate follow-ups (ADR-0042 read-boundary validation, verdict MERGE-with-conditions, 2026-07-06): (1) FOLLOW-UP REQUIRED BEFORE ANY NPM PUBLISH / package-consumer milestone: zod is imported by src/validate.ts (and mcp-server.ts) but NOT declared in package.json dependencies — satisfied transitively via @modelcontextprotocol/sdk whose range permits zod@3, while validate.ts uses zod-4-only API (z.looseObject, top-level .catch). A consumer whose tree hoists zod@3 gets a crash at engine import. Safe today: package-lock pins 4.4.3 and the bundles inline it. Fix = add "zod": "^4.0.0" to dependencies — deferred because npm is offline-blocked here (a hand-edited package.json without a lock refresh would break npm ci); do it on VPN. (2) PERSISTENCE-ON-UPDATE (deliberate, documented in validate.ts): read-boundary defaults become STORED values when a legacy/foreign entry is next edited (update paths spread the normalized entry). Original lines untouched, normalization idempotent — but invalid-but-meaningful original values leave the live record. (3) UNKNOWN ENTRY TYPE COERCES TO 'fact' — the one non-passthrough spot; revisit before any v3 schema adds types. (4) Reviewer measured the zod cost: ~2.4x materialize (0.2→0.5ms at 140 entries; 16→37ms at 10k) — does not undercut ADR-0043's gate, but its eventual index benchmark must baseline against POST-0042 code. (5) CLAUDE.md's "the engine itself is stdlib" is now stale — engine reads through zod; fix on next CLAUDE.md touch.

When onboarding a new vfkb consumer repo (e.g. via a `/new-project`-style bootstrap), always refresh the shared `$VFKB_BUNDLE_DIR` by building from a freshly-checked-out `vfkb` `main` worktree, rather than trusting whatever is already sitting in `$VFKB_BUNDLE_DIR`. Never touch the primary `~/VFKB/vfkb` working directory to do this (no checkout/stash/reset there) — use `git worktree add <scratch>/vfkb-main main` off a plain `git fetch`, reuse `node_modules` from the primary checkout if `package.json` is identical, run `npm run build:bundles` in the new worktree, copy the two bundle files over `$VFKB_BUNDLE_DIR`, then remove the temporary worktree.

Why: The vfkb dev repo routinely has another session mid-work on the v2 branch (or any topic branch) with uncommitted changes at the time a new consumer project is being onboarded. A bootstrap flow that assumes the existing bundle is current, or that checks out/stashes in the primary working directory to get `main`, risks silently wiring a new consumer to stale/wrong engine code or disturbing someone else's in-progress work. Verified live during onboarding of vilosource/viloforge-wiki: `vfkb doctor` confirmed the resulting engine reported `commit 0d4e33c` (vfkb main HEAD) while the primary worktree kept moving independently through v2 commits, untouched.

RFC-021 proposes packaging vfkb as a Claude Code plugin (skill + bundled MCP server + bundled hooks, all resolving the existing harness-agnostic dist/bundles/*.mjs via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}), becoming the primary distribution path for the Claude Code harness face while keeping vfkb init (RFC-010/ADR-0030) as a fallback for CI/scripted consumers. No engine changes — packaging only. Sequenced strictly BEFORE RFC-020 (OKF) per explicit operator prioritization, since RFC-020's proposed vfkb:okf skill depends on this plugin scaffold existing.

Why: Reduces onboarding friction (one /plugin install vs. npm install + manual vfkb init wiring) and gives vfkb a human-facing interactive entry point (a skill) distinct from the MCP tools (agent-facing). Designed with Google Antigravity's independently-converged, near-identical plugin shape (skills+hooks+MCP bundle) in mind, and Pi as a future target, per operator direction to design for future harnesses even where not building for them yet. Acceptance is explicitly gated on a live empirical probe of $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR propagation inside plugin-bundled hooks/MCP servers — a closed upstream GitHub issue (#9447) plus one corroborating comment is evidence, not the observed verification this repo's own culture requires before treating a claim as settled.
RFC-021: vfkb as a Claude Code plugin — primary distribution for the Claude Code harness face: docs/rfc/RFC-021-vfkb-claude-code-plugin.md

RFC-021 Phase 0 empirically verified (CLI v2.1.202, --plugin-dir session-scoped probe, no persistent install): a Claude Code plugin-bundled SessionStart hook correctly resolves both ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR} and ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} when referenced directly in the hook's command string. A plugin-bundled MCP server does NOT inherit either as a general process environment variable (a spawned server reading $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR from its own env sees it unset) — but DOES correctly resolve them when declared as template values inside the MCP server's own "env" field in .mcp.json (e.g. "VFKB_DATA_DIR": "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.vfkb"), which Claude Code substitutes at config-resolution time before spawning. Separately verified: a marketplace.json plugin entry's "source" field can scope to a subdirectory (not just repo root) — a live install test confirmed sibling files (test/, scenarios/, a .vfkb/entries.jsonl stand-in) placed outside the sourced subdirectory did NOT leak into the installed cache (~/.claude/plugins/cache/<marketplace>/<plugin>/<version>/), only the scoped subdirectory's own files did.

Why: This directly gates RFC-021's acceptance (its Phase 0 precondition) and fixes a wrong assumption: naively assuming $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR is inherited process env for MCP servers the way it is for hooks would have shipped a plugin-bundled vfkb-mcp.mjs that silently resolves VFKB_DATA_DIR wrong (or not at all). The subdirectory-scoping result also means vfkb's plugin can ship from this same repo without a separate release branch/tag, avoiding a bigger distribution split than necessary.

CORRECTION to 4c780bd0b89e's scoping claim (found during PR review of the same result). That entry verified "marketplace.json source scopes to a subdirectory" using a LOCAL DIRECTORY marketplace source only (claude plugin marketplace add <local-path>, shown as "Source: Directory" in `claude plugin marketplace list`) — a different code path from a real "Source: GitHub" marketplace. Re-tested directly against vilosource/okf-skill's own real, already-configured GitHub-sourced marketplace (refreshed via `claude plugin marketplace add vilosource/okf-skill#main`, not a synthetic repo): the add command performs a full, UNSCOPED `git clone` into ~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/<name>/ BEFORE any plugin-level source-scoping applies. That clone directory was observed to contain okf-skill's own real .vfkb/entries.jsonl, .claude/settings.json, and full dev tree. `claude plugin marketplace add --help` exposes a `--sparse <paths...>` flag ("Limit checkout to specific directories via git sparse-checkout, for monorepos") specifically because the default is a full clone. The earlier local-Directory-source test only proved the final PLUGIN CACHE (~/.claude/plugins/cache/...) ends up scoped — it said nothing about the MARKETPLACE CHECKOUT (~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/...) that happens first for a real GitHub source.

Why: A same-repo subdirectory-scoped plugin source is NOT sufficient on its own to keep vfkb's own .vfkb/entries.jsonl and dev tree off every plugin consumer's disk -- the full repo lands in the marketplace clone regardless, unless the consumer remembers --sparse. This reverses RFC-021's Phase-0-informed recommendation back toward a dedicated minimal plugin repo/branch (the original pre-Phase-0 contingency), now for a concretely observed reason instead of a hypothetical one. Caught by re-reviewing the same PR that recorded the original (uncorrected) claim -- a reminder that a verified result can still be verified against too narrow a scope.

ADR-0045 — vfkb ships as a Claude Code plugin from a dedicated repo (vfkb-claude-plugin), primary distribution for the Claude Code harness face (accepts RFC-021): docs/adr/ADR-0045-vfkb-claude-code-plugin.md

Replace the engine's hard-coded 'spike' default project name with a shared derivation, storage.defaultProject(): VFKB_PROJECT if set, else the explicit brain dir names the project (parent basename when the dir is dot-named, e.g. <repo>/.vfkb → repo; itself otherwise), else $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR basename, else cwd basename. Used by all render defaults (engine.ts), the CLI hook/resume/context paths (cli.ts), the MCP server (replacing its Track 9 Q0-local projectName()), and the pi extension.

Why: Generic wiring — the ADR-0045 Claude Code plugin — sets VFKB_DATA_DIR but cannot know a project's name, so every plugin consumer rendered project="spike" (verified live 2026-07-07). The brain's location is the one signal generic wiring always carries, so derive the name from it; VFKB_PROJECT stays the explicit override. Verified on the built bundle: plugin-style env renders project="vfkb". Note this only goes live via re-vendor + plugin release (ADR-0045 dev-loop).

ADR-0045 plugin migration changed this repo's dogfooding semantics (PR #75 review finding, recorded 2026-07-07): (1) DEV-LOOP — the live auto-layer now runs the plugin's VENDORED engine copy, not the local build. Editing src/ + `npm run build:bundles` no longer affects what this repo's own sessions run (the pre-plugin $VFKB_BUNDLE_DIR wiring DID pick up local rebuilds). To dogfood an engine change live here, re-vendor + release it in vilosource/vfkb-claude-plugin and update the plugin. Predictable future confusion: "why isn't my engine change live?" (2) UNPINNED — `enabledPlugins: {"vfkb@vfkb": true}` tracks the plugin marketplace repo; plugin updates change this repo's live wiring outside this repo's PR review flow. Accepted trade-off for the first-party plugin. (3) DEGRADATION — the deleted bootstrap.mjs emitted an actionable "vfkb INACTIVE" SessionStart banner when the engine was unresolvable; with the plugin, an uninstalled/unapproved plugin means the session silently runs without vfkb (no banner). All three noted in CLAUDE.md's wiring section (dev-loop bullet added in PR #75).

Plugin wiring VERIFIED LIVE post-restart (2026-07-07, first session on the ADR-0045 plugin): SessionStart injected the resume digest + bundle, PreToolUse blocked a direct .vfkb/ Write, and the plugin MCP server answered kb_map against the repo brain (143 entries). Two cosmetic findings: (1) the resume/context headers render project="spike" — the plugin's hooks.json and .mcp.json set VFKB_DATA_DIR but not VFKB_PROJECT (a generic plugin can't hard-code one), so the engine's hard-coded 'spike' fallback surfaces (src/cli.ts:159,167,327 + engine.ts render defaults). Display-label only — entries are NOT filtered by project, the brain dir is already per-project. Candidate fix: engine derives the default from basename(CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR || dirname(VFKB_DATA_DIR)) instead of 'spike' — note this needs re-vendor + plugin release to go live (ADR-0045 dev-loop). (2) Plugin MCP tools are namespaced mcp__plugin_vfkb_vfkb__kb_*, not the mcp__vfkb__kb_* that CLAUDE.md documents.

Handoff (2026-07-07): three PRs in flight, merge in this order — (1) vilosource/vfkb#75 (chore-migrate-to-plugin: the ADR-0045 migration + live-verification docs), (2) vilosource/vfkb#76 (fix/derive-default-project: storage.defaultProject() replaces the 'spike' label; decision 3ceb8b23a689), (3) vilosource/vfkb-claude-plugin#3 (re-vendor bundles from vfkb@fea3895, plugin v0.1.1 — makes the fix live in plugin consumers). Note: #75 and #76 both append to .vfkb/entries.jsonl on divergent branches — whichever merges second may hit a trivial EOF conflict there; resolve by keeping both lines (the store is merge-union-safe by design). RFC-020 proposes a three-layer knowledge-management split: vfkb owns live/session-integrated Capture (append-only, trust/corroboration-gated), graphify owns read-only rebuildable Understand (structural graph, never originates knowledge, disposable like dist/), OKF owns curated durable/portable Publish (a directory of typed markdown concept docs, zero-tooling readable). Widens Track 9 Q3's queued "AGENTS.md export" render-target work to a second target (an OKF bundle) sharing the same deterministic-projection engine, rather than a separate build.

Why: OKF and graphify were both added to this project with no design record on how they relate to vfkb or each other. Without an explicit layering + directionality rule, three knowledge systems risk triplicated authoring and confidence laundering — e.g. graphify's INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS structural guesses (its extraction has known non-determinism bugs, found this same session) getting silently asserted as flat fact once exported into OKF's confidence-less frontmatter. The one-way ratchet (only vfkb verified+accepted, or graphify EXTRACTED-with-human-review, may cross into OKF) is the load-bearing rule that prevents that.
RFC-020: Layered knowledge management — vfkb (capture) / graphify (understand) / OKF (publish)

Phase 0 SHIPPED with acceptance: all 26 docs/adr+docs/rfc files carry OKF v0.1 frontmatter, both dirs validate strict-conformant. Phase 1 (vfkb export okf) gated on Track 9 Q3; Phase 2 gated on drift-pain evidence. Path: docs/adr/ADR-0046-layered-knowledge-capture-understand-publish.md

RFC-022 → ADR-0047 (Track 9 Q3, ratified 2026-07-08 under operator-directed draft→adversarial-review→merge flow): brain export projections. `vfkb export agents-md` + `vfkb export okf` — two render targets, one projection core; output a PURE FUNCTION of the brain (asOf = max(entry.updated) threaded through the renderers as an acknowledged engine change — current renderers hardcode nowIso(); scoped sweep deleting only generated-marker files, refusing foreign non-empty --out dirs; <id>.md filenames; explicit id tiebreak beyond heuristicCompare). Export predicate fully enumerated (verified ∧ accepted-for-decisions ∧ zone≠archive ∧ validity-open-at-asOf) binding BOTH targets; AGENTS.md uses an export-variant map (live affordances stripped, published-subset counts); .okf/ log.md derives departures from the RAW record history (readRecords-level — materialized LWW view can't prove past publish-grade states). No vfkb:okf skill, no MCP export tool (evidence-gated). BUILD STAYS GATED on operator request; DoD = RED-first unit gate (determinism+refusal, asOf purity, all-clause negative test, log.md history test, TS OKF-conformance port) + validate_okf.py --strict cross-check + L4 agents-md-cold-agent & okf-bundle-cold-agent. Adversarial review (opus agent, 2 rounds): round 1 REDRAFT (2 blockers: log.md on-disk diff broke determinism; asOf claim disguised an engine refactor as reuse) → all 8 addressed; round 2 ACCEPT-WITH-FIXES (5 findings: status flip, archive-zone clause, destructive sweep guard, map-leak, readRecords qualification) → all 5 addressed. ADR-0046's Phase 1 "Q3 drafted" gate is now satisfied.

Why: Q3 was the queued interop slot and ADR-0046's Phase 1 explicitly gated on it being drafted; the operator directed the full draft→review→ratify chain in one pass. The adversarial rounds materially changed the design (log.md rederived from raw records instead of on-disk diffs, the sweep made non-destructive, the export predicate fully enumerated) — evidence the review step earns its cost on RFCs, not just code.

ADR-0048 (2026-07-08, closes issue #82, supersedes ADR-0028): RETIRE scenarios/wiring-smoke.mjs. Its premise — validate this repo's candidate .claude/settings.json before promoting it live — ended with the ADR-0045 plugin migration (live settings = plugin config, no hooks; candidateSettings() synthesized a config nobody runs). Post-migration the gate had NO unique live coverage left (precisely: consumer-onboarding L4 covers the init fallback's SessionStart grounding; decision-capture L4 proves Stop capture against its own synthetic settings; NEITHER host-validates a candidate settings file — that job no longer exists here). SUPERSEDE not amend: ADR-0028's operative mandate (the in-repo gate) is withdrawn entirely, and the relocated check is DEFERRED, not built — the ADR-0028 principle (sandbox-validate wiring by real claude turns pre-release) is re-homed to the plugin repo's release flow as vfkb-claude-plugin#6 (the v0.2.0 vendored verification was one-off manual, not repeatable — an acknowledged, tracked gap until #6 lands). Doc sweep: RUNBOOK §8 promotion instruction replaced, RFC-009 item 6 + roadmap D7-wire got retirement notes, ADR-0028 got the one-line Superseded pointer.

Why: A gate that can only validate a configuration nobody runs misleads on green and blocks for wrong reasons on red; retiring it honestly (retire-and-defer, with the gap named and tracked) beats keeping it or claiming the replacement exists. Framing corrected by adversarial review: the original proposal overstated both the L4 coverage overlap and the plugin-repo relocation as accomplished.

text: ADR-0047 BUILT (2026-07-08, operator-triggered, RED-first + adversarial loop): src/export.ts (~400 lines) + `vfkb export <agents-md|okf> [--out]` CLI verb. Key implementation facts: (1) export predicate = isInjectable(e, asOf, superseded) ∧ verified ∧ accepted-for-decisions — isInjectable already carried clauses 2-4 exactly, evaluated at asOf = max(entry.updated); (2) sweep marker check is LINE-ANCHORED (/^<!-- generated by vfkb export /m) — the adversarial reviewer EMPIRICALLY DESTROYED a copy of RFC-022 through the original substring check (any doc quoting the marker phrase defeated the refusal AND entered the deletion set); (3) log.md derives from raw records with recordWasPublishGrade = predicate MINUS validity clause and superseded set (disclosed refinement: a closed window can never satisfy clause 4 at asOf, so expiry departures would be unloggable; supersession is current-state); (4) disclosed determinism exception: the project label in the H1 comes from VFKB_PROJECT/dir basename (env), everything else byte-pure. EVIDENCE: unit 218/218 (13 export tests, RED observed pre-implementation); dogfood = 130-file bundle from this repo's real brain, validate_okf.py --strict OK, byte-identical re-exports (verified independently by the reviewer); L4 agents-md-cold-agent DEMONSTRATED 3/3 wired vs 0/3 contrast, okf-bundle-cold-agent DEMONSTRATED 3/3 vs 0/3 (host-level claude -p form per the decision-capture.mjs canonical — a disclosed deviation from ADR-0047's 'dockerized' wording; causally sound, the cold arm has no wiring for docker to isolate). Review: round 1 FIX-FIRST (1 MAJOR destructive-sweep + 4 minors) → all fixed/disclosed. ADR-0046 Phase 1 is now fully closed: the whole OKF chain (spec → layering → export) is built and observed working.

NON-NEGOTIABLE DoD GATE (ADR-0050, operator ruling 2026-07-09): no user-facing capability may be declared done or shipped without a full sandboxed, agent-driven L4 — committed reproducible scenario, DEMONSTRATED >=2/3 with a can-fail contrast arm, every load-bearing claim OBSERVED not asserted (a pinned model must appear in modelUsage, a gate must be seen failing). Until that evidence exists and is named, the only honest status is 'built, NOT yet verified'. Release PRs must pass the deterministic release-gate CI Brake. adr,dod,l4,constitutional

Why: The prose-only ADR-0029 rule was observably skipped under end-of-chain momentum: plugin v0.4.0 (a user-facing skill) was released and declared 'closed' on a 1-trial smoke check with no committed record. vfkb's own doctrine: a prose rule with no Brake gets skipped; this decision is constitutional so every session leads with it, and the CI Brake makes skipping it mechanically impossible.

Claude Code plugin updates do NOT arrive on restart, and `claude plugin update` silently targets the wrong scope. Bit for real 2026-07-09: operator restarted expecting /vfkb:brief (plugin v0.4.0) and got "Unknown command: /vfkb:brief" — the session was still on v0.4.0's predecessor. TWO distinct traps, both verified live: (1) a Claude Code restart re-reads the CACHED install (installed_plugins.json -> cache/<mp>/<plugin>/<version>); it never re-pulls the marketplace git clone, which sat pinned at the v0.3.0 merge commit b0e6667 while origin/main had 3aec82f with plugin/skills/brief/SKILL.md. (2) `claude plugin update vfkb@vfkb` DEFAULTS TO `--scope user` and hard-fails with 'Plugin "vfkb" is not installed at scope user' when the install is project-scoped (as vfkb's is here, via .claude/settings.json enabledPlugins). CORRECT SEQUENCE: `claude plugin marketplace update <marketplace>` (refresh the clone) THEN `claude plugin update <plugin>@<marketplace> --scope project` THEN restart to apply. Verify with `claude plugin details <plugin>@<marketplace>` (component inventory lists skills/agents) — note `details` takes NO --scope flag. Post-update installed_plugins.json should show the new installPath + gitCommitSha.

Why: A restart is the operator's mental model for "pick up new plugin code", and it is wrong — nothing in the CLI's success output says the clone is stale. Combined with `update`'s user-scope default failing loudly but unhelpfully on a project-scope install, this cost a full restart cycle and looked like a broken plugin rather than a stale install.

text:
CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is EMPTY in a main-session Bash tool call, but DOES expand inside a plugin skill's / skill-forked subagent's Bash context. Verified 2026-07-09 two ways: (a) `echo "[${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}]"` via the Bash tool in a session with the vfkb plugin active prints `[]` (only CLAUDECODE, CLAUDE_CODE_* and CLAUDE_EFFORT are exported); (b) despite that, the /vfkb:brief L4 (vfkb-claude-plugin scenarios/records/brief-skill.json, pluginVersion 0.4.0) is DEMONSTRATED 3/3 on a predicate that CANNOT pass unless `node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/dist/bundles/vfkb.mjs resume` actually executed: the sentinel codename `copperlark-echo-31` lives ONLY inside the sandbox brain's handoff entry — never in git, never on disk outside .vfkb/entries.jsonl — and the contrast arm (identical sandbox minus the handoff entry) scores 0/3. So the var resolves where skills need it. IMPLICATION: do NOT "fix" a plugin SKILL.md by hardcoding a path or resolving the bundle some other way on the theory that ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} is unset — test it from the skill's own context, not from a main-session Bash probe. The two contexts have different env.

Why: A main-session Bash probe showing an empty CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is a false alarm that would justify a pointless and harmful "fix" to every plugin skill; the L4 sentinel is the ground truth that the variable resolves where it actually matters.
