text:
okf PLUGIN LEAKS A vfkb MCP SERVER (plugin:okf:vfkb) via a STALE marketplace clone — found 2026-07-13, verified live. The okf plugin's marketplace source is the WHOLE repo ("source":"./") and Claude Code auto-loads a plugin's root .mcp.json as plugin:<plugin>:<server>, so okf-skill's OWN dogfooding vfkb wiring (root .mcp.json: `node .vfkb/bin/bootstrap.mjs mcp`, VFKB_PROJECT=okf-skill) rides into every okf install as plugin:okf:vfkb (tools mcp__plugin_okf_vfkb__kb_*) — a redundant 2nd copy of the 9 kb_* tools bound to a relative .vfkb (okf's own empty brain), only connecting because $VFKB_BUNDLE_DIR is set here. OBSERVED: `claude mcp list` in ~/VFKB/viloforge-infra (okf enabled project-scope) shows 'plugin:okf:vfkb: node .vfkb/bin/bootstrap.mjs mcp - ✔ Connected'. ALREADY FIXED UPSTREAM: okf-skill main no longer has the root .mcp.json (migration okf-skill#1, 2026-07-13, removed it — remote 404s the file); this machine's marketplace clone is STALE (local 64c72bb 2026-07-07 vs remote 137d22b), so it still ships the old wiring. FIX on this machine: `claude plugin marketplace update okf-skill && claude plugin update okf@okf-skill` + restart → plugin:okf:vfkb disappears, leaving only the legit plugin:vfkb:vfkb. Textbook RFC-024 §1 / vfkb-doctor stale-marketplace-clone case. SIDE NOTE (unverified, offered next): viloforge-infra also shows its own 'vfkb: ... Pending approval' project-level .mcp.json — check whether it's fully on the plugin or still bootstrap-wired.

Why: A real, live-verified instance of the stale-clone class the project has been building doctor/RFC-024 to catch — the mechanism (source:./ + auto-loaded root .mcp.json = dev wiring leaking into the shipped plugin) is a packaging lesson for every first-party plugin, and the exact refresh command + the already-fixed-upstream status save the next session the rediscovery.

text:
vfkb plugin CONSUMER can be settings-wired yet NOT loaded: OpenIntegrations had correct .claude/settings.json (extraKnownMarketplaces.vfkb + enabledPlugins vfkb@vfkb, byte-identical to working SudoGM) but ZERO kb_ tools and no SessionStart injection in a live session. Root cause: ~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json had NO project-scoped record for that projectPath. enabledPlugins only ENABLES an already-installed plugin; it does not register/install one. The install record is normally created by the first interactive claude + one-time approval in the project; if that never completed post-migration, the plugin is inert despite perfect settings. Fix: run 'claude plugin install vfkb@vfkb --scope project' from the repo (creates the record; no interactive approval needed on this machine). Detection MUST be a live probe (kb_ tools present? SessionStart injection? Stop-hook decision-capture nudge?) — inspecting settings.json is NOT sufficient (ADR-0051 quiet-failure class). Implication: 'N/N plugin-wired' handoffs measured from settings presence overcount actual-loaded consumers.

Why: A migrated consumer looked fully wired but vfkb was completely dead there; the gap (missing installed_plugins.json record) is invisible from the repo and only shows in a live session, so any consumer-wiring audit must probe live, not read config.

text:
RESOLUTION (2026-07-13) of the settings-wired-but-not-loaded plugin failure (see gotcha 8e76f8f72b64): it was WIDESPREAD, not just OpenIntegrations. A live audit of all 8 vfkb-plugin-declaring repos found 5 INERT (no installed_plugins.json project record, so zero kb_ tools and no SessionStart injection): OpenIntegrations, vilonotes, vfkb-claude-plugin, okf-skill, viloforge-wiki (plus the wt-vfkb-rfc worktree). Only vfkb and SudoGM were actually loaded. Root cause of the class: an AGENT/automated migration PR can write the .claude/settings.json enabledPlugins declaration but CANNOT run the interactive plugin-install step, so the migration lands settings-only and is silently inert; the prior 10/10-plugin-wired claim was measured from settings presence, which overcounts. FIXES APPLIED: (1) live install records restored for all 5 via claude plugin install vfkb@vfkb --scope project (verified: vilonotes went from inert to kb_ tools loaded). (2) INACTIVE guard (ADR-0059, vfkb-guard.mjs) swept into all 7 real repos, one PR each: OpenIntegrations #29, SudoGM #2, vilonotes #58, okf-skill #2, viloforge-wiki #8, vfkb #160, vfkb-claude-plugin #17. (3) MIGRATION_GUIDE hardened in plugin #17: a Step-1 warning that plugin-install is interactive, a Step-4 verify-it-loaded checkpoint (resume digest + kb_ tool + vfkb doctor plugin line), and the guard reframed as the automated backstop. NOTE: vfkb doctor ALREADY detects this at doctor.ts line 540 (WARN: enabled in settings but not found in the local plugin registry); the gap was that nobody ran it, and the guard now makes the check automatic every session.

Why: Documents the full breadth and resolution so a future session does not re-treat this as an OpenIntegrations-only one-off; the 5-of-8-inert observation is the evidence that settings-presence audits overcount and live probes are mandatory.

text:
Autonomous PR workflow (operator grant 2026-07-14): when handling a PR, do NOT stop to ask for merge approval — launch an adversarial review, fix any real findings, re-review, iterate up to 3 rounds; merge autonomously on convergence (green/clean); escalate to operator (HITL) only if it does not converge after 3 tries. Still never commit directly to main/v2; still branch->PR->merge; still report clickable URLs on push. Release PRs whose merge triggers an outward publish are covered by the grant but the publish consequence must be called out when reporting.

Why: Operator wants throughput on the PR queue and trusts the adversarial-review+fix loop as the quality gate instead of a per-PR manual approval stop; this is the explicit autonomous-mode call-out that the branch->PR->merge self-merge latitude requires (CLAUDE.md commit rules).
why: Operator wants throughput on the PR queue and trusts the adversarial-review+fix loop as the quality gate instead of a per-PR manual approval stop; this is the explicit autonomous-mode call-out that the branch->PR->merge self-merge latitude requires (CLAUDE.md commit rules).

text:
okf STALE-CLONE fix recipe CORRECTION (supersedes the recipe in gotcha 7dc5f817f108): 'claude plugin marketplace update okf-skill && claude plugin update okf@okf-skill' is INSUFFICIENT when the upstream fix is a FILE REMOVAL with no version bump. Observed 2026-07-14: marketplace update DID refresh the clone (64c72bb->137d22b, dropped the root .mcp.json), but 'plugin update okf@okf-skill --scope project' reported 'already at the latest version (0.1.0)' and DID NOT re-copy the cache — the stale leaked file survived at ~/.claude/plugins/cache/okf-skill/okf/0.1.0/.mcp.json (Jul-7). Because Claude Code auto-loads a plugin's CACHE-dir root .mcp.json, the redundant plugin:okf:vfkb server persisted. FIX that actually worked: delete the stale cache file directly (rm ~/.claude/plugins/cache/okf-skill/okf/0.1.0/.mcp.json) then /reload-plugins or restart. Safe because the marketplace source no longer contains it, so no legitimate re-copy restores it; a full uninstall+reinstall would also work but risks disturbing the fragile project-scope install record (see gotcha 8e76f8f72b64). Also note: okf plugin is project-scope, so 'plugin update' needs --scope project (default is user -> 'not installed at scope user' error).

Why: The recorded refresh recipe silently leaves the leak in place whenever the upstream fix is a fileremoval without a version bump — 'plugin update' short-circuits on the version match; the durable fix is to clear the cache artifact directly.

text:
viloforge-wiki gets NO ADR-0059 INACTIVE guard — leave it on its existing bootstrap wiring (operator ruling 2026-07-14, option A). Rationale: viloforge-wiki main is BOOTSTRAP-wired (.vfkb/bin/bootstrap.mjs hooks in .claude/settings.json), NOT plugin-wired — it declares no enabledPlugins[vfkb@vfkb]. The ADR-0059 guard keys off that declaration (exits silently when absent), so it would be an inert dead file there; and bootstrap already emits its own ADR-0031 'vfkb INACTIVE — set VFKB_BUNDLE_DIR' banner. PR #8 (the guard sweep's viloforge-wiki arm) was therefore CORRECTLY closed unmerged. A future session must NOT re-add the guard to viloforge-wiki; the only path that would make it applicable is a full plugin migration, which is a separate deliberate decision to be coordinated with the other agent that also works in that repo.

Why: Prevents a future session from mis-reading 'viloforge-wiki declares the plugin but lacks the guard' (the stale-audit framing that opened this) and re-adding an inert guard; records that closing #8 was correct and that only a full plugin migration would change the calculus.
why: Prevents a future session from mis-reading 'viloforge-wiki declares the plugin but lacks the guard' (the stale-audit framing that opened this) and re-adding an inert guard; records that closing #8 was correct and that only a full plugin migration would change the calculus.

text:
NO upstream feature request for the enabled-but-unloaded-plugin gap (operator ruling 2026-07-14). We will NOT file the Claude Code feature request that ADR-0059 Decision §3 / RFC-032 layer 3 contemplated ('surface enabled-but-unloaded project plugins at session start'). Rationale: Claude Code is closed-source (only a public issue tracker at anthropics/claude-code exists), so an upstream ask is slow and low-leverage; the committed ADR-0059 workaround — the engine-free repo-side vfkb-guard.mjs INACTIVE guard — already covers the observed failure modes. Consequence: the guard is now the PERMANENT mitigation, NOT an interim measure 'pending upstream' as ADR-0059 §3 framed it (this amends that one sub-point). This CLOSES the 'UPSTREAM FEATURE REQUEST' open follow-up from handoff 8fbbad797978 — a future session must NOT file it or re-raise it unless the operator reopens the question.

Why: Closes an open follow-up so it is not re-litigated every session, and reframes the guard from 'interim workaround pending upstream' to the accepted permanent mitigation; records the closed-source reality (issue tracker only) that makes the upstream route low-value.

NEW CONSUMER + SKILL FIX (2026-07-14): bootstrapped vilosource/ViloGate (private) — the FIRST repo wired to vfkb on the plugin mechanism FROM SCRATCH (not migrated from bootstrap). ViloGate = broker-agnostic open-source AI governance/runtime control plane (K8s-native on kagent, Kafka-API-compatible); created from PRD v1.0; stage=architecture-blueprinting, docs-first scaffold. Wiring = .claude/settings.json (extraKnownMarketplaces.vfkb + enabledPlugins vfkb@vfkb) + .claude/vfkb-guard.mjs (byte-identical to vfkb main), brain seeded via engine CLI. Live-verified: guard fires the INACTIVE banner (plugin declared-but-not-installed = can-fail proof) + brain round-trips. Manual step pending on ViloGate: 'claude plugin install vfkb@vfkb --scope project' + restart. ALSO FIXED the vfkb-new-project skill (~/.claude/commands/vfkb-new-project.md, a personal command — NOT version-controlled): steps 2/5 rewritten from legacy $VFKB_BUNDLE_DIR bootstrap to plugin wiring + guard install + the two live verifications + the settings-wired!=loaded caveat (gotcha 8e76f8f72b64). Partial progress on #161 (commented); still open there: vfkb init guard scaffolding + CONSUMER-ONBOARDING docs still describe the old ADR-0031 bootstrap guard.

Why: Records the first from-scratch plugin-wired consumer and the skill fix so the plugin-onboarding procedure that actually worked is captured; flags that the skill lives outside version control and that #161's in-repo surfaces (vfkb init, onboarding docs) are still unbuilt.

text:
Issue #161 (scaffold ADR-0059 guard into onboarding) RESOLVED as docs-only (PR #168, merged d9752ed, closes #161). Rewrote docs/CONSUMER-ONBOARDING.md + CONSUMER-ONBOARDING-PROMPT.md to lead with the plugin/ADR-0059 guard wiring (the two committed files fetched byte-for-byte from vfkb main: .claude/settings.json + .claude/vfkb-guard.mjs), the one-time `claude plugin install vfkb@vfkb --scope project`, and the ADR-0059 INACTIVE signal; the legacy $VFKB_BUNDLE_DIR bootstrap flow is demoted to a clearly-labelled Fallback section. NOT touched: src/init.ts. Rejected #161's literal item (1) "make `vfkb init` drop the guard" because `vfkb init` scaffolds BOOTSTRAP wiring (.mcp.json + bootstrap.mjs + hooks) and never writes enabledPlugins, so the ADR-0059 guard (which keys off enabledPlugins) would be PERMANENTLY INERT there (same as the recorded viloforge-wiki#8 finding) — dead code. The skill path was already done: the vfkb-new-project skill scaffolds the guard for new plugin consumers (ViloGate #167 got it for free). OUT OF SCOPE / future: whether `vfkb init` itself should be reworked to scaffold plugin wiring (become the canonical plugin-era onboarding CLI) is an RFC-sized question, not built.

Why: The issue (filed 2026-07-14, before the skill fix and the viloforge-wiki#8 internalization) partly conflicted with recorded architecture: the skill path was already scaffolding the guard, and a guard added to `vfkb init` would be inert dead code because the guard's detection keys off enabledPlugins which the bootstrap wiring never declares. The only genuinely-stale surface was the onboarding docs still teaching the pre-plugin bootstrap paradigm as the default — which actively misleads a new consumer. Docs-only fix is DoD-exempt (ADR-0029), verified for factual accuracy by an adversarial review (CONVERGED, 3 minor accuracy nits fixed).

OBSERVED, not asserted: (1) `claude plugin tag [path]` exists — creates annotated `{name}--v{version}` at HEAD, validating plugin.json<->marketplace.json agree. (2) All six shipped versions retro-tagged + pushed to origin: vfkb--v0.1.0(92a1bc1) v0.1.1(1b06eed) v0.2.0(8650e7e) v0.3.0(d00fa32) v0.4.0(79d2758) v0.5.0(e7550fd=HEAD), each tag's tree plugin.json matches the tag. (3) HERMETIC PROOF (isolated CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR): `claude plugin marketplace add vilosource/vfkb-claude-plugin@vfkb--v0.4.0` clones at that ref -> resolves plugin.json 0.4.0, records {source:github,repo,ref:vfkb--v0.4.0}. So a pushed tag IS a resolvable marketplace version = the RFC-024 §4 upgrade-arm prerequisite. FINDING: v0.5.0 had drifted — the INACTIVE guard (#16) + hooks-smoke L4 (#15) shipped under 0.5.0 with NO bump; retro-tag blesses this (no renumbering shipped history), policy prevents new drift going forward. Cost is NOT a constraint (operator directive: thoroughness over budget). Delivery STILL unproven until the record lands (ADR-0051 disclosure unchanged).

text:
install-path L4 upgrade mechanics — PROVEN hermetically 2026-07-16 (isolated CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, no metered turns), plus two traps. SEQUENCE THAT WORKS: (1) `claude plugin marketplace add vilosource/vfkb-claude-plugin` (unpinned=tracks latest) clones SHALLOW; (2) rewind to model a stale consumer: `git -C <clone> fetch --unshallow` THEN `git checkout <previous-release-commit>` (the shallow clone means unshallow is REQUIRED first, per RFC-024 §4); (3) `claude plugin install vfkb@vfkb --scope user` installs the rewound version; (4) advance: `git checkout main` in the clone + `claude plugin marketplace update vfkb`; (5) `claude plugin update vfkb@vfkb` -> prints 'Plugin vfkb updated from 0.3.0 to 0.5.0. Restart to apply changes.' and installed_plugins.json installPath+version flip to 0.5.0 IMMEDIATELY on disk. TRAP 1 (stale-cache): `marketplace add` pre-caches the LATEST version (a cache/vfkb/vfkb/0.5.0 dir appears) even when you then install an older version — so a filesystem 'is there a brief dir anywhere in cache' check is MISLEADING. The authoritative signal is installed_plugins.json's installPath/version (the LOADED plugin) and, for the L4, a LIVE `claude -p` turn invoking /vfkb:brief (sentinel + haiku in modelUsage) — never a cache scan. TRAP 2 (restart): `plugin update` says 'Restart to apply'; on-disk state flips immediately, so a FRESH `claude -p` process (a new turn()) should load the new version — this is RFC-024's 'least-evidenced step', now on-disk-confirmed but only fully certified by the Phase-2 metered post-update turn. Capability boundary: /vfkb:brief absent in v0.2.0/v0.3.0, present v0.4.0+; upgrade arm pair = v0.3.0 -> v0.4.0/0.5.0. Tags (ADR-0060) let install-path.mjs resolve 'previous release' as the 2nd-newest vfkb--v* tag durably instead of a rotting SHA.

Why: These mechanics + traps are exactly what would burn metered Phase-2 sessions to rediscover (a wrong cache-based predicate would produce a FALSE proof — worse than none). Recording them observed-not-asserted so install-path.mjs is built right the first time: live-turn predicate, unshallow-before-rewind, stale-cache-aware.

text:
ADR-0061 accepted (2026-07-16) — plugin bump-and-tag is enforced mechanically, not by prose. Authored HERE (docs/adr/ADR-0061-plugin-release-version-automation.md); IMPLEMENTED in vilosource/vfkb-claude-plugin (scenarios/version-bump.mjs + .selftest.mjs, .github/workflows/release-tag.yml, RELEASING.md). Invariant: a shipped version is immutable — if the consumer surface (plugin/ + templates/) differs from what vfkb--v{version} shipped, bump. The tag is created on merge, so it cannot be forgotten. Corrects ADR-0060: the real v0.5.0 drift was ONE file (templates/vfkb-guard.mjs, #16) — no post-bump commit touched plugin/, and the hooks-smoke L4 (#15) was never drift. release-please rejected (right for THIS engine repo per ADR-0056, wrong there): its TAG_PATTERN cannot parse back vfkb--v0.5.0 (round-trips to component 'vfkb-'), chore-only re-vendors cut no release, and its force-pushed release PR would clobber the metered L4 records. Full detail in the plugin repo's brain (entry 77b2b2baae8c).

Why: ADRs live centrally in vfkb (per the plugin repo's CLAUDE.md), so this brain must know ADR-0061 exists and that release-please was evaluated and rejected for the plugin repo — without which a future agent re-proposes it.

CORRECTION to the v0.5.0 "drift" claim in ADR-0060 and in decision 27e3b2e1124c (both mine, both WRONG on the facts). Verified 2026-07-16 by reading plugin commits 0fc7a88..e7550fd: (1) `plugin/` — the INSTALLED artifact — is BYTE-IDENTICAL across all four post-bump commits (no installed-plugin drift at all); (2) hooks-smoke #15 (f569e1d) touched only scenarios/ + RELEASING.md and changed NO consumer byte — it was NEVER drift (ADR-0060 wrongly listed it); (3) the ONLY real drift is a single file, templates/vfkb-guard.mjs (+96 lines, from the INACTIVE guard #16 e7550fd), which consumers copy+commit (ADR-0059). ADR-0061 carries the authoritative correction (ADR-0060's body stays immutable per ADR-0001). This matters because the naive Brake "if plugin/ changed, bump" would have been GREEN on the very commit that motivated it — the version-bump Brake correctly diffs plugin/+templates/ against the vfkb--v{version} tag.

Why: Verification-first + acknowledge-when-wrong: my ADR-0060 and Phase-0 decision asserted a drift narrative that reading the diffs disproved. Recording the corrected facts so the brain doesn't keep propagating the error; the surface set (plugin/+templates/) and the tag-based Brake depend on getting this right.

plugin#22 CLOSED (2026-07-16, PR vfkb-claude-plugin#27 merged): the delivery proof is now TREE-BOUND — a record cannot claim a tree it did not test. Three observed layers: (1) REF-pinned installs (`marketplace add owner/repo@<ref under test>`, default=current pushed branch; pre-flight fails loud on dirty plugin/, gitignored files inside plugin/, detached HEAD, unpushed/diverged ref, ambiguous ls-remote — all before any metered turn); (2) in-predicate content assertion (fresh:treeVerified, upgrade:treeVerifiedAfter — hashTree(installPath) must equal hashTree(local plugin/), with the cache-root .in_use bookkeeping marker excluded and one 2s settle-retry, both fail-closed); (3) gate-side tree-binding (records carry pluginTreeHash; checkDelivery re-derives hashTree(plugin/) — collision-safe framing: NUL-terminated path + fixed-width content digest — and rejects divergent or hash-less records; closes the gap version-binding can't see: a 2nd plugin/ change under an already-bumped unreleased version). Process: adversarial review CONVERGED, all 4 minors fixed+observed; 3 metered runs total (2/3-with-flake, then clean 3/3 after the .in_use fix — the shipped scenario is the one that produced the shipped record); gate selftest 74/74; delivery stays proven on main. FOLLOW-UP FILED: plugin#28 — capability records (brief-skill/hooks-smoke/inactive-signal) remain version-bound only, the #22 class survives for them (reviewer's scope-gap finding, disclosed not implied). QUEUE (umbrella plugin#26): next = #23 (engine-change → auto re-vendor PR; needs operator-provisioned PAT), then #24 (unattended evidence, ADR-ratified; kagent endpoint existence unconfirmed), then #25 (auto-merge policy). KNOWN RUNTIME FACTS for #23+: hashTree exported from release-gate.mjs is the engine-staleness comparison primitive candidate; `.in_use` is Claude Code cache bookkeeping; annotated tag objects never equal commit shas (ls-remote needs ^{} peeling).

Why: First issue of the release-automation queue closed with the full honesty mechanism recorded; the runtime facts (hashTree primitive, .in_use marker, tag peeling) were each paid for with probes or a burned trial and directly shape #23/#24 implementations.

Design: producer-side single workflow (vfkb engine-delivery.yml) — build bundles, compare STAMP-NORMALIZED bytes (scripts/bundle-drift.mjs, exit 0/1/2; ENGINE_COMMIT/ENGINE_VERSION esbuild defines neutralized with \\d* rename tolerance) against plugin-vendored copies; on drift push re-vendor/engine + open the release PR with PLUGIN_DISPATCH_TOKEN (user PAT ⇒ CI runs). Headless claude -p already runs in-cluster (a2a-harnesses claude-code image; a2a-chat-skeleton agent/run.py). Egress currently OPEN (ingress-only NetworkPolicies; lockdown planned). NO metered ANTHROPIC_API_KEY path seeded yet (both harness+skeleton accept either); NO CI-invokes-agent example exists anywhere yet; viloforge-infra docs mention no vfkb. DESIGN IMPLICATION for #24, two layers: (A) execution = a job on the arc-a2a-harnesses in-cluster runner (claude CLI + node + git image, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY via Vault/ESO) running the 4 L4 scenarios directly from the release branch — simple, deterministic, reuses live substrate; (B) agency = a kagent BYO evidence-runner agent (vilonotes-researcher pattern) invoked over A2A for diagnose/retry/escalate, with Tempo tracing — the fleet-vision layer, later. Operator context: kagent is in RESEARCH phase right now — #24 design should not hard-depend on kagent maturity; layer A stands alone.

Why: Grounds plugin#24's biggest open question (where does unattended evidence run) in observed manifests rather than assumption: the mesh-only endpoint kills the naive GitHub-hosted-runner-calls-A2A design, the ARC scale set provides the bridge, and vilonotes-researcher proves the Claude-harness-on-kagent pattern already works in this cluster.

vfkb doctor double-wiring WARN is a FALSE POSITIVE on any repo carrying the ADR-0059 INACTIVE guard: doctor.ts:483 classifies a hook as old vfkb wiring via substring JSON.includes('vfkb'), and the guard hook command 'node .../.claude/vfkb-guard.mjs' contains 'vfkb'. Observed 2026-07-17 on vfkb itself AND on freshly-migrated viloforge-asdlc (all old wiring removed, plugin 0.6.0 installed) — both show 'vfkb hooks present ALONGSIDE the plugin' with zero actual old hooks. Do NOT follow the WARN's 'remove them' advice on such repos (it would strip the ADR-0059 guard); the authoritative all-clear is the 'plugin — vfkb@vfkb installed, version …' OK line. Fix tracked as a vilosource/vfkb issue (tighten predicate to bootstrap.mjs/cli-hook markers, exclude vfkb-guard.mjs).

Why: Doctor is the authoritative wiring check per MIGRATION_GUIDE.md, and this WARN's literal advice tells an agent to delete the exact backstop the same guide mandates — a future session following doctor verbatim would undo ADR-0059 on every consumer repo.

OPERATOR INTENT (2026-07-17): cross-repo operations should leave a record in each affected repo's OWN brain — expressed after the plugin-0.6.0 consumer sweep, where per-repo handoff facts were written into 11 consumer brains via the working mechanism: VFKB_DATA_DIR=<repo>/.vfkb node <vfkb>/dist/cli.js add fact "…" --tag handoff,cross-repo,plugin,distribution (the engine CLI is the sanctioned cross-repo write path; the session MCP kb_add binds only to the local brain). Operator: "I like this idea or feature for cross repo operations, lets expand and discuss this intent." This is the WRITE-side complement of RFC-013/ADR-0038 cross-project brain query (read side). First real evidence exists (the sweep + the viloforge-wiki two-agent collision that per-repo records would have prevented); per evidence-gated-build discipline this is now RFC-worthy: provenance convention (structural origin marker vs tag convention), transport (CLI env-var vs a `vfkb broadcast` helper vs MCP tool param), commit semantics (leave to target repo vs ADR-0033-style pathspec-scoped commit), trust defaults (arrive unverified). Next free RFC number at time of writing: RFC-031.

Why: The operator explicitly asked to expand and discuss this as a feature; recording the intent + the working mechanism + the design axes means the eventual RFC session starts from the evidence instead of re-deriving it.

text: RFC-033 cross-repo brain write (operation record broadcast) — the write-side complement of ADR-0038; proposed 2026-07-17, PR #187, amended per independent review PR #189

ADR-0063 RATIFIED (operator, 2026-07-17): cross-repo brain write — a session that deliberately changes another repo's observable state FIRST consults that repo's brain (ADR-0038 read side), then leaves EXACTLY ONE fact in it through the engine, tagged cross-repo (NEVER handoff/next — those claim the resident's ADR-0049 continuity pin; the motivating sweep's handoff-tagged entries hijacked all 11 target pins before being retagged), text opening with 'CROSS-REPO <operation> (<date>, from <origin>)'. Delivery = a second bounded pinned section '## Cross-repo operations' (ADR-0049 mechanics) built with `vfkb broadcast --to <dirs>`; until it ships, v1 delivery is disclosed best-effort. Write-never-commit (entry rides the target's ADR-0033 discipline; broadcast reports per-target commit posture). Arrives unverified. Rejected by name: MCP-side targeting, auto-broadcast, handoff-pin delivery, any global store. DoD = L4 cross-repo-record, three arms (unpressured v1 arm = convention's gate; pressured delivery arm = RED-first contract for the pin/broadcast build; sentinel-keyed contrast arm). Accepted from RFC-033 after two independent review cycles (PRs #187, #189).

Why: The 2026-07-17 sweep proved both halves live (11 repos changed with no brain-side record; the wiki collision from unread resident state), and the independent review proved the naive version is actively harmful (the handoff tag evicts resident continuity) — so the convention, its delivery channel, and its DoD are ratified together rather than left as improvisation.

Model claude-haiku-4-5, live metered host-form (session-start-briefing.mjs precedent). PLUGIN v0.7.0 RELEASED + FULL CONSUMER SWEEP COMPLETE (2026-07-17). The ADR-0062 automation worked end-to-end on its first real engine change: engine-delivery.yml detected bundle drift on vfkb main@9aa13dc and auto-opened plugin PR #32 (born RED on the evidence gate, by design). Release flow executed per RELEASING.md: four L4s re-pinned from the pushed re-vendor/engine branch, all DEMONSTRATED — brief-skill 3/3 (sentinel+haiku observed), hooks-smoke 3/3 (all six observables), inactive-signal 3/3 (banner iff absent), install-path 3/3 fresh + 3/3 upgrade with contrast arm observed failing ('Unknown command'); delivery stays PROVEN. Consumers must restart open sessions to load 0.7.0.

vfkb broadcast --to with a comma-joined list: shell tilde expansion only applies to the FIRST item (~ expands at word start only), so '--to ~/A,~/B,~/C' passes literal '~/B','~/C' which broadcast resolves relative to cwd → refused as 'no brain' with a misleading path like /cwd/~/B/.vfkb. Observed live 2026-07-17 on the first production broadcast (10/11 targets refused on the first attempt). Use absolute paths (or $HOME) for every item in the comma list. Two real engine findings from the same run, both filed: vilosource/vfkb#193 (broadcast permanently refuses wired-but-manifest-less plugin-born brains — only 'vfkb init' ever calls writeManifest(); heal by engine-writing the manifest: node -e "import('./dist/manifest.js').then(m=>m.writeManifest('<target>/.vfkb'))" — used to heal ViloGate) and vilosource/vfkb#194 (broadcast's auto 'cross-repo' tag not deduped against user --tag → duplicate tag in written entries).

Local bundle-drift checks on THIS machine are a false positive: ~/VFKB/vfkb node_modules is stale (esbuild 0.21.5 installed vs package.json ^0.28.1, node v20.18.1 vs CI node 22 — the corporate-mirror bootstrap, see CLAUDE.md env caveat), so 'node scripts/bundle-drift.mjs dist/bundles <vendored>' reports DRIFT against bundles CI built even when they're correct. To verify a re-vendor PR's bundle integrity, read the engine-delivery.yml run log: its drift step prints the FRESH hashes it built (e.g. 2026-07-17 run 29597609864: fresh ce01c2271627/4e2296fa0b80) and those must equal the hashes the PR vendors — observed match = verified. Do not 'fix' a re-vendor PR from a local build off this toolchain.

NEAR DATA LOSS 2026-07-17 (self-inflicted, recovered): ran 'git reset --hard' in ~/VFKB/vfkb-claude-plugin while its working tree held 2 uncommitted .vfkb/entries.jsonl lines (the 0.6.0 cross-repo record 1c75b2b12c62 + its retag revision — one of the sweep records deliberately left uncommitted in target trees). Unstaged working-tree changes are NOT in git's object DB — no fsck/stash recovery. Recovered because the originating vfkb session transcript (~/.claude/projects/-home-jasonvi-VFKB-vfkb/*.jsonl) contained the verbatim add command + tool output; restored via engine as fb4926425871 (rode plugin PR #32). STANDING RULES: (1) before any reset --hard/checkout -f in ANY consumer repo, check git status for uncommitted .vfkb changes — broadcast/cross-repo records park uncommitted on main BY DESIGN until a topic-branch brain commit, so consumer trees routinely hold unique brain data; (2) session transcripts are the recovery source for lost brain writes.

SESSION HANDOFF (2026-07-17, session 3): PLUGIN v0.7.0 RELEASED AND DELIVERED — handoff item (a) complete. The ADR-0062 auto re-vendor PR (plugin #32, from vfkb main@9aa13dc) was found open as designed, born RED; all four L4s re-pinned DEMONSTRATED from the pushed branch (brief-skill 3/3, hooks-smoke 3/3, inactive-signal 3/3, install-path 3/3+3/3 upgrade, contrast arms observed failing), gates green, merged 1de934e, tag vfkb--v0.7.0 auto-created (observed via ls-remote), delivery PROVEN. Full consumer sweep: 12/12 project-scope installs at 0.7.0 (registry-verified); cross-repo records delivered to all 11 consumer brains via the FIRST LIVE 'vfkb broadcast' (10 written 2nd attempt after the tilde-path gotcha 30c394b2b7a9; ViloGate needed a manifest heal first — engine findings filed as vfkb#193 manifest-less-brain refusal and vfkb#194 tag dedup). Incident: 2 uncommitted plugin-brain lines discarded by a careless reset --hard, fully restored from session transcript as fb4926425871 (gotcha e3199efc468e). NEXT: (a) operator: restart open Claude Code sessions so consumers load 0.7.0 (this vfkb session itself still ran 0.6.0); (b) #193/#194 are small evidence-backed engine fixes, queue per roadmap; (c) plugin queue unchanged (#24 unattended evidence, #25 auto-merge policy, #28 tree-bind records); (d) release-please #165 (vfkb npm 0.3.0) still open; doctor false-positives #186/#188 and brain-loss window #175-177 still open; (e) vfwb 9dbdf4a + viloforge-infra 98d8dd1 migration commits still unpushed on their in-flight branches; broadcast records (0.6.0 retagged + new 0.7.0 set) park uncommitted in consumer trees BY DESIGN — do not sweep-commit them from outside; each repo's next session commits its own brain on a topic branch.

ADR-0063 §3 'never bootstrap a wire-less brain' is keyed on ENTRIES, not the manifest (fix for vfkb#193): a target with entries.jsonl but no manifest.json is a WIRED brain (plugin-born — only 'vfkb init' ever calls writeManifest), so broadcast heals it (engine-writes the manifest, visibly: healed flag in the result + CLI line) and delivers; a target with no entries.jsonl is truly wire-less and stays refused with nothing created. This is a bug-fix reading of the accepted ADR's intent (the manifest check was a false proxy for wiredness), not an amendment — the invariant the ADR protects (no brain is ever bootstrapped by a visitor) is preserved and now guarded by a dedicated unit test. Also fixed alongside: vfkb#194, the auto 'cross-repo' tag is deduped against user --tag.

Why: Observed live 2026-07-17 (first production broadcast): ViloGate's real, session-bearing brain was permanently unreachable by cross-repo writes because its birth path (plugin auto-layer) never writes a manifest. Refusing wired brains inverts §3's purpose — the gate exists to prevent creating brains where none exist, not to strand brains the engine itself created.

text: REVIEW GATE (ADR-0052) on PR #196 (fix/broadcast-193-194 vs ADR-0063 §3): round 1 = FIX-FIRST — 1 major + 2 minor. Major: the new heal path called writeManifest outside any try/catch, so one unwritable target (EACCES) threw and aborted the WHOLE broadcast, discarding already-delivered results — a defect the PR itself introduced, violating the module's own per-target-loudness contract; probe-demonstrated with a good-target-first ordering. Minors: healed flag dropped on heal-then-failed-write (silent stamp); wire-less refusal reason still pointed at manifest.json (nudging operators toward a forbidden hand-write). All fixed in 4f10701 with three revert-checked guard tests (each observed RED against the round-1 code). Round 2 = MERGE, probes re-executed at head, 324/324. Record: reviews/4f10701*.json. Reviewer also flagged (non-blocking): the chmod-based guard test breaks if the suite ever runs as root (chmod no-op) — guard with process.getuid()===0 skip if a root container enters the matrix.

Bundle integrity observed: vendored vfkb.mjs stamp-normalized hash b76a921654dd == CI's fresh hash at bd11887 (computed via bundle-drift's own fingerprint(), NOT a raw sha256sum — those are not comparable); vfkb-mcp.mjs unchanged (4e2296fa0b80), correct since the fix touched no MCP inputs. Process note: the ADR-0052 record must be the TRAILING commit on the PR branch — a brain-entry commit after it broke the review-gate sha strip-walk (CI red), fixed by soft-resetting the brain commit off and parking the entry for brain-sync (precedent: PR #191/#192 pattern).

(2) #193/#194 broadcast fixes — vfkb PR #196 (RED-first tests, 2-round ADR-0052 gate, 324/324) → auto re-vendor plugin PR #33 → v0.8.0 released same-day, 12/12 consumers updated, broadcast 11/11 with both fixes observed live. Issues #193/#194 CLOSED by merge. Evidence chain fully committed: reviews/4f10701*.json (vfkb), scenarios/records/*@0.8.0 + DELIVERY-STATUS proven (plugin). REMAINING QUEUE (unchanged priorities): brain-loss/MCP-disconnect cluster #175/#176/#177 (highest stakes, likely RFC-shaped); doctor false-positives #186/#188; release-please #165 (vfkb npm 0.3.0 — merging PUBLISHES to npm, call it out); plugin queue #24 (blocked on operator ADR ratification)/#25/#28; vfwb 9dbdf4a + viloforge-infra 98d8dd1 still unpushed on in-flight branches. OPERATOR: restart open Claude Code sessions to load plugin 0.8.0. Parked-by-design: 0.7.0+0.8.0 broadcast records sit uncommitted in consumer trees until each repo's own next topic-branch brain commit — do NOT sweep-commit or reset them away (see gotcha e3199efc468e).

ADR-0062 engine-delivery cycle: ALWAYS delete the plugin repo's re-vendor/engine branch when merging its PR (use 'gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch'). Observed 2026-07-18: PR #33 (v0.8.0) was squash-merged WITHOUT --delete-branch; the stale branch survived carrying the records commit, and the NEXT cycle's engine-delivery run (for vfkb merge a710bc7, the #199 render fix) FAILED with 're-vendor/engine is STALE vs vfkb main but carries non-automation work (1 foreign commit, 4 record files) — refusing to force-push over committed evidence'. The guard is correct (vfkb#182 review finding — it protects committed L4 evidence from being clobbered); the remedy is: verify the stale branch is content-identical to main (git diff origin/main origin/re-vendor/engine --stat → empty, guaranteed after a squash-merge of that branch), delete it (git push origin --delete re-vendor/engine), then gh run rerun the failed engine-delivery run.

SESSION CLOSE (2026-07-18): #175/#176/#177 cluster fully dispatched. Release PRs (release-please standing PRs, e.g. vfkb "chore(main): release X.Y.Z") are covered by the standing autonomous-PR grant — do NOT hold them for operator approval and do NOT re-review their content. Every constituent commit already passed the ADR-0052 review gate before landing on main; the release PR is pure downstream bookkeeping (version bump + changelog) whose merge triggers the release chain. Standing procedure when the batch is complete: (1) push a user-authored empty commit to the release branch to fire required checks (bot PRs get no workflow runs); (2) merge once green; (3) release-please creates the tag + GitHub Release; (4) dispatch publish.yml with the tag by hand (bot tags fire nothing); (5) watch prove → publish → canary to green; (6) report with the outward-publish callout. The only judgment left is cadence — "is this batch complete?" — not approval.

Why: Operator ruling 2026-07-18: asked why release PRs keep being surfaced as awaiting action — "shouldn't a release PR be automatically accepted, since it means everything has been through the review gates already?" Correct: the content is reviewed pre-merge on main; holding the release PR adds no review value, it only delays distribution (npm 0.3.0 lagged plugin 0.10.0 for exactly this reason). The outward-publish guardrail is a call-out-when-reporting duty, not an ask-first duty (brain decision 5f380d6ca496).

JOURNAL-GITIGNORE SWEEP (surveyed 2026-07-18, handoff item (b) made concrete): of the 13 local repos with a .vfkb/ brain, only vfkb, OpenIntegrations and now EventShield ignore .vfkb/.journal/. STILL MISSING (10): SudoGM, ViloGate, okf-skill, vfkb-claude-plugin, vfwb, viloforge-asdlc, viloforge-infra, viloforge-wiki, vilonotes, wt-vfkb-rfc. Impact is bounded: the SessionEnd auto-commit is pathspec-scoped to entries.jsonl so the automatic path never commits the WAL — only the manual 'git add .vfkb' that CLAUDE.md documents does. Fix is one line next to the existing .vfkb/index-meta.json / .sessions/ / .signals/ ignores; verify with 'git check-ignore -v .vfkb/.journal/wal.jsonl' and confirm .vfkb/entries.jsonl is NOT caught (it must stay tracked). EventShield done in its PR #2 (a379392), which also landed 7 parked broadcast records now that adding .vfkb/ is safe there.

Why: The handoff named this as a to-do but not which repos; a session picking it up would have to re-survey. Recording the exact remaining list plus the verification command makes it a mechanical sweep rather than a rediscovery, and records the bounded-impact finding (auto path safe, manual path not) so nobody treats it as urgent data loss.

JOURNAL-GITIGNORE SWEEP COMPLETE 2026-07-18 — supersedes the to-do in b67183d06c17. All 10 remaining consumers fixed: EventShield (PR #2, also landed 7 parked records), SudoGM #3, ViloGate #1, okf-skill #3, vfkb-claude-plugin #37, vfwb #21, viloforge-asdlc #52, viloforge-infra #79, viloforge-wiki #11, vilonotes #59 — all merged. Method that avoided disturbing in-flight work: cut each branch from origin/main in a THROWAWAY git worktree (git worktree add -b <branch> <tmp> origin/main), edit there, push, PR, then worktree remove — never checked out or switched a branch in the live clone, so parked entries.jsonl records and feature-branch work were untouched. wt-vfkb-rfc was correctly EXCLUDED: it is a worktree of vfkb itself (not a separate repo) on branch rfc/branch-aware-brain-visibility; vfkb main already carries the ignore, so it inherits on merge. Verification predicate per repo (all passed): check-ignore resolves the WAL, .vfkb/entries.jsonl is NOT ignored, `git ls-files | git check-ignore --stdin` is EMPTY (nothing tracked becomes hidden), diff is exactly +1/-0. RESIDUAL GAP: .vfkb/.lock is ignored in vfkb's own .gitignore (line 13) but in NONE of the 10 consumers — same defect class, deliberately NOT folded in because it is LATENT not live (the lock is transient, held only during a write; observed absent on disk in all 10, unlike wal.jsonl which persists in every one). Also noted: ViloGate is the only consumer where .vfkb/manifest.json is untracked; the other 9 commit it.

Why: Closes handoff item (b) with the exact PR list and, more importantly, records the throwaway-worktree method — the reusable technique for any cross-repo sweep over clones that have in-flight branches or parked brain records, which is the normal state of this fleet. The .lock residual is recorded with its triage rationale so a future session neither re-discovers it nor treats it as urgent, and the ViloGate manifest anomaly is captured before it is forgotten.

text:
.vfkb/manifest.json IS a committed file (ADR-0030, src/manifest.ts header: "a small COMMITTED file … distinct from the derived/gitignored index-meta.json") — it stamps which engine version a brain targets so a consumer cannot silently bind to a stale/incompatible engine. But the widely-copied .gitignore comment in consumer repos says "only .vfkb/entries.jsonl is committed", and vfkb's own CLAUDE.md says the same — BOTH ARE INACCURATE and actively dangerous: reading them literally invites someone to gitignore the manifest. That is how ViloGate ended up the only consumer without an engine stamp (file written to disk, never git added; it was never ignored, just never committed). Fixed 2026-07-18 in ViloGate PR #2, whose second commit also corrected that repo's comment to "entries.jsonl + manifest.json are committed". The comment remains wrong in the other 9 consumers and in vfkb's CLAUDE.md — cosmetic there only because those repos already track the manifest. Correct committed set = entries.jsonl + manifest.json; correct ignored set = index-meta.json, .sessions/, .signals/, .journal/, .lock.

Why: A doc line that contradicts the code is worse than no line: this one already caused a real defect (a repo with no engine stamp) and would cause it again, because the natural remedial action on seeing an untracked manifest.json is to gitignore it rather than commit it. Recording the authoritative committed/ignored sets here means the next session resolves it from the brain instead of trusting the wrong comment.

text:
SUBAGENT FINDINGS ARE EVIDENCE, NOT CONCLUSIONS — verify before promoting one into a DURABLE artifact (GitHub issue, ADR, brain entry, commit message, PR body), regardless of its severity label. Observed failure 2026-07-18: the ViloGate PR-2 review returned a MINOR finding about manifest engine_commit="dev" containing one uncited sentence ("writeManifest() re-stamps on the next engine write") that was FALSE; I relayed it into vfkb issue #212 and amplified it into a fix proposal ("don't overwrite a real sha") for a code path that does not exist. Ground truth (grep -rn writeManifest src/, ~5 seconds): only TWO callers — init.ts:154 and broadcast.ts:111 — and the broadcast call is guarded by if(!existsSync(manifestPath)), i.e. heal-on-absence only, never an overwrite. Three transferable checks: (1) a causal claim about WHEN code runs is a claim about WRITERS — enumerate call sites; definition-site + consumption-site citations never establish causation, and that was exactly the gap; (2) in an otherwise well-cited report, the UNCITED sentence is the one to check — the asymmetry is a visible tell; (3) gate verification on ARTIFACT DURABILITY, not on severity: a MINOR finding correctly does not block a merge, but that same label must not lower the bar for publishing it, because a wrong issue misdirects a future engineer while wrong chat text is correctable next message. Root mechanism: the merge-evidence bar was applied rigorously (all 9 sweep diffs re-verified by hand) and then dropped for the incidental finding, because filing it FELT like diligence.

Why: This is CLAUDE.md's own standing rule ("VERIFIED = observed, not asserted — never relay a gate's/agent's verified as fact without reading ground truth") failing in the specific place it is easiest to miss: not on the main deliverable, where the discipline held, but on a tangential finding being captured in passing. Recording the mechanism (severity label mistaken for an evidence bar; correct citations lending credibility to an uncited inference) so the rule attaches to artifact creation, which is where the cost lands, rather than only to merge decisions.

DURABLE-CLAIM BRAKE SHIPPED + OBSERVED LIVE 2026-07-18 (PR #213, merge 8feebde) — the mechanical answer to "how do we stop the verification rule lapsing", replacing the prose-only gotcha a1aea707436c. scripts/hook-durable-claim-check.mjs is a PreToolUse nudge firing when a claim becomes an ARTIFACT (gh issue/pr create|comment, body-bearing edit|review|release, writes to docs/adr, docs/rfc, reviews/, CLAUDE.md), silent otherwise. VERIFIED END-TO-END: it fired on the very next `gh issue create` (#214) from merged main — observed in-session, not asserted. THE REVIEW CAUGHT THE BRAKE ITSELF SHIPPING THE DEFECT IT EXISTS TO PREVENT: v1 wrote the reminder as BARE PROSE to stdout, which the harness ignores, so it was INERT — a PR about announcing properties your code lacks, announcing one. Correct contract, verified by direct execution + two in-repo precedents (src/cli.ts:702-710; the live graphify hook-guard): {hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:'PreToolUse',additionalContext:'…'}}. Worse, the selftest asserted only NON-EMPTY STDOUT — orthogonal to whether the harness surfaces it — so the inert version passed three watched mutations. Lesson compounding the original: A MUTATION TEST ONLY PROVES THE AXIS IT ASSERTS ON; "watched it go red" is not "watched it go red for the property that decides whether the feature works". Selftest now parses and asserts the shape, and is watched failing on a bare-prose mutation. Also amended .claude/commands/review.md step 7 (causal claims must enumerate call sites; unverified claims labelled UNVERIFIED; severity governs blocking, never the evidence bar). Open minor F4 (stdin never closing may stall rather than fail open) filed honestly as #214 — shared with vfkb's own hook I/O convention (src/cli.ts:698), not introduced here.

Why: Records both the Brake and the far sharper lesson its own review produced: a selftest can be watched failing three times and still be blind to the one property that makes the feature work. That is the generalisation of the original miss — the first was "I did not verify the claim", this is "I verified the wrong axis and mistook it for coverage". Also documents the working PreToolUse additionalContext contract, which cost a review round to establish and is not obvious from vfkb's own cli.ts (which emits permissionDecision, a different shape).

ROADMAP RE-RATIFIED 2026-07-18 → P10 "trust-and-cadence" phase (PR #215, merge de11c68, docs/H4-DEVELOPMENT-ROADMAP.md §4). Order: P10-a DOCTOR TRUST CLUSTER (#186 double-wiring WARN, #188 manifest message, #206 journal-gitignore outside-repo, #212 dev-sentinel drift) — one file, one PR, unit-proof only (doctor is a structural invariant; ADR-0029 "proof fits the capability" means NO metered L4) → P10-b RELEASE CADENCE (#211/0.4.1 deliberately HELD: package.json ships files:['dist'] and that release moves no dist/ byte, so publishing would push a byte-identical artifact under a new number; hold until dist/ moves, then roll up — cadence is the ONE judgment the no-approval rule of 872c1ff0ff90 leaves) → P10-c ADR-0065 write-health (§0 Tier-0 MCP-disconnect probe first; BLOCKED on a tracking gap — the handoff names #176, which is CLOSED, so the initiative has no open issue) → P10-d plugin release automation (#25 under umbrella #26 — P10-b settles it in policy, #25 makes it mechanical: the same prose→Brake move as ADR-0050). Rationale for P10-a going first: doctor is what a consumer runs to learn whether their wiring is healthy, and a check that cries wolf trains people to ignore it — the same desensitisation argument the durable-claim Brake's design rests on. Also marked §1's status snapshot (2026-06-28, "95/95 unit", pre-v2) STALE IN PLACE rather than leaving the roadmap contradicting itself; suite is 345/345.

Why: The previous Current action ("build the v2 backbone on v2") shipped 2026-07-08 (PR #86, 5bb087e), so for ten days the execution authority named a finished frontier while the real work was maintenance-shaped (four plugin releases, npm 0.3.0/0.4.0, ADR-0064). §4's own protocol says the "what's next?" urge is the signal to re-ratify rather than answer ad hoc — this is that protocol firing. Recording the REASONING per step, not just the order, so the sequence survives a session boundary: a bare ordered list would not carry why #211 is held or why P10-a needs no L4.

text:
P10-a DOCTOR TRUST CLUSTER SHIPPED 2026-07-18 (PR #216, merge 372bab1; review record reviews/8956c7d…json, 2 rounds, verdict MERGE). Closed #186 (double-wiring WARN: the predicate was JSON.stringify(hook).includes('vfkb'), so the ADR-0059 guard the MIGRATION_GUIDE tells consumers to commit was classified as old wiring and doctor advised DELETING the INACTIVE detector — replaced by isEngineWiring(), which recognises an actual engine hook-dispatcher invocation), #188 (no-manifest message claimed 'stamped on the next write'; writeManifest has only two callers and the append path is not one), #206 (check-ignore exits 128 for a brain outside the work tree, read as 'not ignored' — now gated on repo-toplevel containment via realpath boundary compare). #212's DOCTOR half fixed (engineDrift: a sentinel on EITHER side means 'cannot compare'); its STAMPING half stays open. THE REVIEW'S REAL LESSON — the same vacuity that produced the durable-claim Brake bug, recursed: my #212 tests called the pure predicate directly and never inspected a DoctorReport, so replacing the call site with `if (false)` — DELETING drift detection from the shipped tool — left 30/30 GREEN; and the #188 arm asserted only negatives through `c?.detail ?? ''`, which also hold when the check DISAPPEARS, so gutting the message left 15/15 green. Both reproduced by hand before fixing. FIX PATTERN WORTH REUSING: test at the altitude of the SHIPPED artifact (runDoctor / rendered CLI output), not the extracted helper; a helper test is necessary but never sufficient. Required a new injectable seam DoctorOpts.engineCommit, because ENGINE_COMMIT is a module constant equal to the 'dev' sentinel under the tsc/test build — the branch was literally unreachable from a test. Proof: 15 unit tests RED-first, every fix reverted in isolation with its own guard observed red, over-suppression guarded in both directions, 367/367, #186 re-verified live on this repo (the canonical reproducer) and the #212 provenance notice verified reaching the terminal through the real CLI.

Why: Records the first P10 item as done with its evidence, but more importantly captures the recurring failure mode in its second concrete instance: a test that asserts an extracted helper rather than the shipped behaviour passes when the feature is deleted. Two independent occurrences in one day (the Brake's bare-stdout selftest, then this) make it a pattern worth naming rather than a one-off — the remedy is to pick the assertion's ALTITUDE deliberately, and to prove it by deleting the feature and watching the suite go red.

text:
P10 SESSION CLOSE 2026-07-18 — what shipped and what the day actually taught. SHIPPED: npm 0.4.0 + 0.4.1 published (canary green); roadmap re-ratified to the P10 trust-and-cadence phase (#215, de11c68); P10-a doctor trust cluster (#216, 372bab1 — closed #186/#188/#206, #212's doctor half); P10-c FIRST HALF — ADR-0065 §1 capture-fallback line + §2a MCP loudness floor (#217, c30fefc; §0 probe and §2 doctor write-probe remain UNBUILT, #176 REOPENED because it had been closed when the RFC merged though the ADR says "Decided, NOT yet built"); the durable-claim Brake (#213, 8feebde) + its review.md step 7; a 10-repo consumer sweep for .vfkb/.journal/ then a second for .vfkb/.lock + the false committed-files comment; the init.ts GENERATOR fix incl. heal-in-place on upgrade (#219, e4d6831); viloforge-infra migrated off the bootstrap at last (its PR #80); EventShield/ViloGate brought to parity. STILL OPEN: P10-b needs no action (0.4.1 shipped the dist change), P10-d plugin release automation (#25/#26) NOT STARTED, #214 hook-stdin convention, #220 the false claim in CLAUDE.md:80 + 4 docs, #212's stamping half, plugin re-vendor so the init fix is actually DELIVERED. THE DAY'S REAL LESSON, four independent instances: MY PROOFS KEPT ASSERTING THE CONVENIENT AXIS RATHER THAN THE DECIDING ONE. (1) the Brake's selftest asserted non-empty stdout, so an INERT bare-prose hook passed 3 watched mutations; (2) doctor's #212 tests called the pure predicate, so deleting drift detection from the shipped tool left 30/30 green; (3) the §1 budget test asserted PRESENCE, so making the line budget-droppable passed until a POSITIONAL assertion was added; (4) init's tests asserted SUBSTRINGS, so commenting out every emitted path — a stanza ignoring nothing — passed 3/3. Remedy that worked every time: assert at the altitude of the SHIPPED artifact (rendered CLI output, the built server over the wire, git check-ignore), then DELETE THE FEATURE and watch the suite go red. Corollary discovered twice: a green mutation run has TWO causes — the guard is blind, or the mutation never applied. Always distinguish before concluding.

Why: Closes the session with a handoff, but the load-bearing part is the pattern: four separate features shipped with proofs that could not fail for the reason they existed, each caught by a different mechanism (adversarial review twice, my own mutation once, the operator's question once). One instance is an error; four in a day is a habit with a name and a remedy, and naming it is the only thing that stops the fifth.

text:
WORKFLOW WORKTREE ISOLATION POLLUTES LOCAL TEST DISCOVERY. Agents spawned with isolation:'worktree' get a full checkout under <repo>/.claude/worktrees/<runId>-N. Those are NOT auto-removed when the agent changed files (the tool only auto-cleans an UNCHANGED worktree), and vitest happily discovers test files inside them. Observed 2026-07-18 after a 3-agent workflow: `npm test` on merged main reported 162 files / 1722 tests — roughly 4x the real 42 / 468, because the same suite was being counted from the main checkout plus three leftover worktrees. Every test passed, so the run LOOKED like unusually thorough evidence rather than a broken measurement. Fix: `git worktree list` then `git worktree remove --force .claude/worktrees/<...>` and `git worktree prune`, then re-run. Detection heuristic: a sudden multiple-of-N jump in Test Files is worktree duplication, not new coverage — compare against the count CI reports, since CI checks out clean and is therefore the authoritative number. Related trap already on record: a green run has more than one possible cause, and the flattering one is rarely the right guess (2eee8912cf0c).

Why: An inflated-but-green suite is the worst shape of bad evidence: it reads as MORE confidence, not less, so nobody investigates. Recording the arithmetic tell (an N-fold jump in file count matching the number of spawned worktrees) because that is what actually made it noticeable, and the cleanup command because the next person will otherwise re-derive it mid-session.

DELIVERED 2026-07-18: plugin v0.11.0 shipped and INSTALLED on all 12 consumers — the day's engine work now actually reaches them. Chain: vfkb npm 0.5.0 published (canary green) -> bundles rebuilt from main@aeaa5fa -> vendored into the plugin -> 0.10.0->0.11.0 -> all four version-bound L4 records RE-PINNED live (metered) -> release gate + version Brake green in CI -> PR #40 merged f907feb5 -> release-tag.yml auto-created vfkb--v0.11.0 (verified on origin by git ls-remote, not assumed) -> marketplace update + per-project `claude plugin update vfkb@vfkb --scope project` across all 12. VERIFIED AT THE REAL SURFACE: installed_plugins.json reports 12/12 at 0.11.0, and the DELIVERED bundle at ~/.claude/plugins/cache/vfkb/vfkb/0.11.0/dist/bundles/vfkb.mjs greps positive for all three engine changes (provenance-unknown notice, capture-fallback line, .vfkb/.lock). L4 results: inactive-signal absent 3/3 present 0/3; brief-skill wired 3/3 contrast 0/3 with claude-haiku-4-5 seen in modelUsage (ADR-0049 pin OBSERVED); hooks-smoke wired 3/3 on all six observables, unwired 0/3 on four; install-path fresh 3/3 upgrade 3/3 contrast 0/3 returning "Unknown command: /vfkb:brief" — the can-fail arm genuinely failing. GOTCHA WORTH REUSING: `claude plugin update vfkb@vfkb` alone FAILS with 'not installed at scope user' — these installs are PROJECT scope, so the update must run per-repo from inside each project dir with --scope project. A marketplace refresh alone does NOT pull the new version into the cache. TRIVIA CLOSED: inactive-signal's absent arm shows the ADR-0059 guard banner reaching the agent as "SessionStart:startup hook success: vfkb INACTIVE …" — so a SessionStart hook's BARE stdout IS injected (unlike PreToolUse, which needs hookSpecificOutput JSON). The suspicion that the guard was inert across 11 repos is dead, confirmed twice: official docs + this live harness.

Why: Closes the delivery loop that ADR-0051 exists to keep honest: a release is not delivery, and delivery is not proven until the artifact a consumer actually loads is inspected. Records the per-project scope gotcha because the obvious command fails in a way that reads like the plugin is missing rather than like wrong scope, and records the SessionStart-stdout answer so nobody re-opens that question a third time.

OPERATOR RULING 2026-07-19: RFC-036 (machine-produced release evidence) is DEFERRED — not rejected. Consequence: plugin #24 stays open and unratified, #25 (a green release PR merging itself) stays blocked behind it, and the #26 automation umbrella stalls at its last two items. Release evidence therefore continues to be produced BY HAND on the operator's machine — four metered L4 re-pins per plugin release (~12 metered sessions for install-path alone), exactly as done for v0.11.0 on 2026-07-18. That cost is now an accepted, recurring, per-release tax rather than a defect to route around. Nothing about what counts as proof changes: ADR-0050/0051 stand unmodified, and the RFC's "what must NOT change" section (real sessions, sandboxed HOME, no --plugin-dir, version- AND tree-bound records, can-fail arm observed failing) remains the standard whenever this is revisited. P10-d in the roadmap is consequently PARKED; the P10 phase's remaining live work is plugin #28 (tree-binding the three capability records) and the ADR-0065 remainder (#176 §0 probe then §2).

Why: A deferral that lives only in a chat message is indistinguishable from an oversight three sessions later, and the roadmap actively names P10-d as live work. Recording it as a decision — with the recurring manual cost stated plainly, since that is what the deferral actually buys and pays — so a future session neither re-proposes it as new nor treats the hand-run re-pins as a process failure.

text:
BUILD STATUS DOES NOT BELONG IN AN ADR — and ADR-0064 already proves it. ADR-0064 (durable-capture journal) carries a "Status honesty (ADR-0050/0051)" section reading "Decided, NOT yet built". It WAS built, shipped as plugin 0.10.0, and delivered to all 12 consumers on 2026-07-18 — so that line has been FALSE since the day after it was written, and ADR-0001 makes ADRs immutable, so it cannot simply be corrected. ADR-0065 carries the same section and will rot the same way the moment §0/§2 land. Only 2-3 of 65 ADRs do this, all recent, so it is a new habit rather than an established convention. THE CATEGORY ERROR: an ADR's status field tracks the DECISION's lifecycle (Proposed/Accepted/Superseded) — "Accepted" means "we decided this", never "we built this". Build status is mutable project state with a different lifetime; putting it in an immutable document guarantees rot. It is also redundant: the tracking issue, docs/H4-DEVELOPMENT-ROADMAP.md §4, and brain status entries all already carry it and are all allowed to change. THE PATTERN THAT WORKS, one floor down in the same project: plugin delivery status lives in DELIVERY-STATUS.json, a separate MUTABLE file whose value release-gate.mjs DERIVES from the committed evidence and refuses when hand-edited. Same honesty intent, but machine-checked and permitted to change. Prose in an immutable ADR has neither property. RECOMMENDATION: ADRs state decision status only; if a reader must be warned, POINT at the tracker ("build tracked in #176") rather than assert a state the document cannot keep current.

Why: The disclosure instinct behind these sections is correct and constitutional (ADR-0050/0051, "silence is the violation") — which is exactly why the failure is easy to miss: it looks like rigour. Recording the concrete counter-example (ADR-0064 stale within 24 hours) because an abstract argument about ADR hygiene would not survive contact with that instinct, whereas a document in the repo that currently states the opposite of reality does.

text:
ADR-0065 §0 PROBE RESULT 2026-07-19 (CLI 2.1.215, claude-haiku-4-5) — A CRASHED MCP SERVER AND A HUNG ONE ARE NOT THE SAME DEFECT, AND ONLY THE HUNG ONE IS #176. Observed, not assumed, via scenarios/probes/mcp-disconnect.mjs (a proxy over the REAL dist/mcp-server.js that SIGKILLs it after the 1st tools/call, so death is deterministic and caused by the agent's own activity rather than racing a timer). MODE=kill (process EXITS): Claude Code TRANSPARENTLY RESPAWNS the stdio server — two complete lifecycles ~17s apart observed in the probe's own server log, the second serving the retry — both writes landed, no error, turn completed normally. NOTHING IS LOST; building recovery for this would be engine theatre. MODE=hang (process ALIVE, never answers): only 1 of 2 kb_* tool calls ever returned a result, the second write NEVER LANDED, NO error surfaced, and the agent's whole turn WEDGED until the 300s harness timeout (spawnSync claude ETIMEDOUT). That is the success-shaped nothing #176 describes. CONSEQUENCE FOR §2, and it inverts the obvious design: LIVENESS != HEALTH — a check asking "is the MCP process running?" reports HEALTHY in the exact case that loses data, because the process is alive and merely mute. §2's doctor write-probe must test RESPONSIVENESS via a bounded round-trip, and must carry its own timeout or it inherits the same hang. HONEST LIMITS: N=1 per mode, one model, one CLI; the hang column comes from the transcript + the brain file, both independent of the agent's self-report (it never answered, having timed out). UNVERIFIED: whether repeated crashes exhaust the respawn appetite, and whether a server dying DURING a call behaves like either column.

Why: §0 existed precisely because the failure shape on the current stack was unknown, and the result overturns the intuition the ADR was drafted under: the dramatic-sounding failure (server crashes) is the harmless one, while the boring one (server answers nothing) is the data-losing one. Recording the discriminator now because §2's design hinges on it — a liveness check would have been the natural build and would have reported healthy in the only case that matters.

text:
ADR-0065 COMPLETE 2026-07-19 — all buildable sections shipped, #176 CLOSED, §3 filed external-blocked as #229. §0 Tier-0 probe (#227 de37f09a: scenarios/probes/mcp-disconnect.{mjs,md} + 2 records) · §1 injected CLI-fallback line and §2a MCP loudness floor (#217 c30fefc) · §2 doctor 'write-health (filesystem)' (#228 492d12c1). BOTH SURFACES VERIFIED LIVE ON MAIN. THE HEADLINE: §0 INVERTED THE DESIGN. A CRASHED MCP server is benign — Claude Code respawns it transparently (3 distinct spawns, 3 distinct pids, 63.5s) and both retry and write succeed. A HUNG one (alive, mute) is the real #176: call never returns, write lost, no error, turn wedged to timeout. So LIVENESS != HEALTH — the obvious build (doctor checks the MCP process is running) reports HEALTHY in the exact case that loses data. Without the probe I would have built that. THE §2 DELIVERABLE IS THE WORDING, NOT THE PROBE: doctor is the CLI face and CANNOT see the failure that loses writes, so a TRUE 'ok' is what a reader over-reads into 'my capture is fine'. The review defeated my first guard by growing the line to 'capture is reaching disk' while the suite stayed 7/7 GREEN — gotcha 6ad98196b5a2 reproduced INSIDE the PR that cites it, my own test defending the overclaim. Fixed by asserting SHAPE not a phrase list: any clause raising capture/kb_*/MCP must carry a negation; verified the reviewer's mutation AND a fifth wording I never enumerated both go red. NOT CLAIMED, deliberately: §3's silent-swallow is external-blocked not fixed, and its 'no error surfaced' is bounded by a 300s probe timeout (NOT 'none ever'); the probe is Tier-0 N=1 per mode, recording a contract not proving a capability; doctor's read-back is defensive but UNGUARDED, untestable without ADR-0044's storage seam.

Why: Closes the last live item of the P10 phase, but the durable value is the §0 inversion: the dramatic failure (crash) was harmless and the boring one (mute) was the data-losing one, so probe-before-design earned its cost in a single run. Recording the §2 wording defeat alongside it because that is the second time in one day a proof passed by becoming more confident rather than more true, and the remedy that worked — assert shape, not enumerated phrases — generalises past this ADR.

text:
GRAPHIFY DOC RE-EXTRACTION IS DESTRUCTIVE-BY-REPLACEMENT, NOT ADDITIVE — a refresh can DELETE coverage (observed 2026-07-22). graphify's build_merge does replace-on-re-extract (#1344): every source_file present in the new extraction is dropped from the base graph before merge. So a doc pass that extracts THINNER than the accumulated prior extraction does not "update" those docs, it REPLACES rich nodes with poor ones. Observed concretely: `graphify update .` (AST/code-only, free, no LLM) correctly refreshed the code layer — graph.json went 2192 -> 2452 nodes, built_at_commit b76aef1 (Jul 17) -> a4a4831 (HEAD), picking up src/journal.ts (44 nodes) and doctor write-health (22 nodes) that were absent. But the follow-up SEMANTIC doc pass over 131 changed docs (66 ADRs + 37 RFCs + the rest) via graphify.llm.extract_corpus_parallel(backend='gemini', gemini-3-flash-preview, default settings) yielded only 171 raw / 166 deduped nodes — ~1.3 nodes per doc. Merging that would have taken the graph 2452 -> 1630 nodes (-822): docs/adr 456 -> 78 (-378), docs/rfc 336 -> 37 (-299), docs/other 203 -> 151. The prior doc nodes were accumulated across multiple earlier sessions and are far richer than one default flash pass. ABORTED before any write; graph.json never touched (build_merge only READS it). Cost of the wasted pass: 324,635 input / 24,124 output Gemini tokens. CLEANUP REQUIRED AFTER ABORTING, because two side effects persist and would poison a retry: (1) save_semantic_cache had already written 125 thin entries under graphify-out/cache/semantic/ — deleted by mtime, keeping the 103 older rich ones; (2) save_manifest had already stamped the current file state, so a later --update would report "nothing changed" and silently hide the gap — restored from the graphify-out/<date>/ backup. UNVERIFIED: graphify's own shrink guard (to_json refuses to write a smaller graph, #479) is documented and would plausibly have blocked the write at Step 4, but I stopped before Step 4 so I never observed it fire — do not rely on it as the only backstop. NET STATE: code layer fresh at HEAD, doc layer still at its pre-Jul-17 extraction (so ADRs 0063/0064/0065 have no doc nodes).

Why: ADR-0046 makes graphify the Understand layer and calls graphify-out/ "disposable like dist/" — which invites treating a rebuild as free and always-safe. It is not: for the semantic/doc half, a rebuild is a REPLACE, so a weaker extractor silently ratchets coverage DOWN, and the failure is success-shaped (the pass exits 0 and reports nodes extracted). This is the same quiet-success trap ADR-0051 §3 names, and it needs a content assertion — compare per-directory node counts old vs new BEFORE writing — not an exit code. Recording the concrete numbers and the two persistent side effects so the next session can retry the doc pass with a stronger extractor instead of rediscovering this at the cost of another metered run.

text:
GRAPHIFY DOC RE-EXTRACTION — ROOT CAUSE FOUND, AND IT IS NOT THE MODEL (corrects the remedy implied by 1a9911bd0585; that entry's replace-on-re-extract mechanism stands). RESOLVED 2026-07-22. The vfkb doc graph's ~456 docs/adr + ~336 docs/rfc nodes were NEVER LLM output: they are STRUCTURAL MARKDOWN HEADING nodes produced by graphify.extract.extract() — filename node (L1), title heading, then one node per '##' section (Context/Decision/Consequences/Alternatives). That is why every ADR scored a suspiciously uniform 6-7 nodes and why all 456 carry confidence=None (an LLM pass would tag EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS). Proof: running extract() over 10 ADRs regenerated the old nodes BYTE-IDENTICALLY — same ids, labels, source_locations, 6.1/doc vs the 6.6 baseline. THE REAL BUG WAS OPERATOR ERROR IN HOW THE PASS WAS RUN: the skill's Step 3 sends CODE through Part A (structural, free) + docs through Part B (semantic, LLM), and only code gets BOTH. Feeding docs to the semantic path ALONE means build_merge's replace-on-re-extract swaps the heading skeleton FOR concept nodes instead of stacking them. The fix is additive: run extract() over the .md files too, then merge structural + semantic exactly as the code path does. CHASING A STRONGER MODEL WAS A DEAD END, DISPROVEN BY PILOT: gemini-3-flash-preview gave 1.3 nodes/doc, deepseek-v4-pro with deep_mode=True gave 2.5 — vs a 6.6 'baseline' that was never the same KIND of node. A 10-doc pilot cost 8.4k in / 14.5k out and killed the wrong hypothesis for ~1/15th the price of the full run it prevented; do this before any full doc pass. FINAL RESULT (written, verified): 2572 nodes / 4148 links at built_at_commit a4a4831, docs/adr 456->520, docs/rfc 336->364, total 2452->2572, ADR-0063/0064/0065 and RFC-024/025/026 all present. TWO STANDING QUALITY CAVEATS ON LLM DOC EXTRACTION HERE: (1) deepseek-v4-pro FABRICATES source_file paths — 37 of 379 nodes (~10%) named files absent from disk, several near-miss transpositions of real ADR filenames (ADR-0041-merge-union-entries.md for the real ADR-0041-entries-merge-union.md; ADR-0004-decision-entry-type.md for ADR-0004-decision-is-adr-grade.md), so ALWAYS filter semantic nodes on (root/source_file).exists() before merging; (2) the built graph carries 422 dangling-endpoint edges (~9% of 4757) whose endpoints were never created — same hallucination class, surfaced by graphify.diagnostics, non-fatal but real.

Why: The first entry recorded the mechanism correctly but pointed the next session at the wrong fix — 'retry with a stronger extractor' — which would have burned another metered run to reach the same 2.5 nodes/doc wall. Recording the actual discriminator (structural heading nodes vs semantic concept nodes are DIFFERENT KINDS, so comparing their per-doc counts is apples-to-oranges) because the density gate that caught the regression also nearly produced the wrong diagnosis: the number was right, the interpretation was not. Also recording the pilot-before-full-run habit and the two hallucination caveats, since both are properties of this corpus + backend that will recur on every future doc pass.

text:
OPERATOR RULING 2026-07-22: do NOT file the graphify defect report upstream. Wait, and re-check later whether safishamsi/graphify has fixed them independently. Four defects were found and drafted against graphifyy 0.9.8 while rebuilding vfkb's Understand layer (ADR-0046): (1) doc re-extraction is lossy by construction — the skill's Step 3 Part A collects detect['files']['code'] only, so markdown never gets structural extraction while Part B replaces its nodes (cost us -822 nodes: docs/adr 456->78, docs/rfc 336->37); (2) `graphify hook install` can write OUTSIDE the repo through a symlink — hooks.py _hooks_dir() applies its repo-containment check only on the local-.git/config branch, and when core.hooksPath is set GLOBALLY that branch is skipped entirely, falling through to `git rev-parse --git-path hooks` which honours the global setting and gets no containment check at all; _install_hook() then writes through the symlink, and on this machine `post-commit` symlinks to a shared `_forward` dispatcher that eight hook types across every repo point at; (3) the shrink guard counts NODES ONLY, so a rebuild losing 318 links at -4 nodes writes through clean; (4) merge dedup DROPS a collapsed node's edges instead of rewiring them to the survivor — caused by an id-convention collision between the two extractors for the same document (structural `docs_adr_adr_0001_record_decisions_as_adrs` vs semantic `docs_adr_ADR-0001-record-decisions-as-adrs`). Artifacts preserved OUT of the ephemeral session scratchpad to ~/.claude/notes/graphify/: defects-0.9.8-UNFILED.md (the complete unpublished draft), tooling-roadmap-2026-07-22.md, and RECHECK.sh (compares installed graphifyy version against the observed 0.9.8 and enumerates what to re-test per defect). EVIDENCE QUALITY IS NOT UNIFORM, and this must survive with the report: (1) and (3) are strongly observed; (2) is a code-path reading plus a sandbox proof of the symlink-write primitive, but `hook install` was NEVER actually run, so the end-to-end claim is inference joining two verified pieces; (4) is 100% correlation (all 153 lost edges incident to a dropped node, 0 of 60 dropped nodes in the incoming extraction) but its MECHANISM was never confirmed in source — nobody read the dedup function to verify it discards rather than remaps. Confirm (4) before any future report.

Why: None of the four blocks vfkb: the local wiring already routes around all of them — a hand-written guarded .git/hooks/post-commit (never `graphify hook install`), the corrected doc-pass procedure in CLAUDE.local.md, and a SessionStart staleness reporter. Filing is unpaid work on a third-party tool, is outward-facing and effectively irreversible, would disclose this machine's global hook layout and repo shape, and defect (2) is security-shaped enough that public full-disclosure is the wrong default for what looks like a solo-maintainer project. Recording the version pin and the uneven evidence quality because the whole point of waiting is that a future session must be able to tell "upstream fixed it" apart from "we never proved it in the first place".

SESSION HANDOFF 2026-07-22 — GRAPHIFY (ADR-0046 Understand layer) MAINTENANCE ONLY; no vfkb code, docs, ADRs or roadmap were touched, and the P10 handoff's "do NOT invent work" instruction was honoured. TRIGGER: a question — "are we using graphify for vfkb development?" — which surfaced that the graph was 51 COMMITS STALE (built_at_commit b76aef1, 2026-07-17) while the machine-local hook-guard fired "MANDATORY: run graphify query first" on every single tool call. WHAT LANDED, all machine-local and gitignored (nothing in this PR but brain entries): (a) graph rebuilt and CURRENT — 2572 nodes / 4148 links at built_at_commit a4a4831 == HEAD, docs/adr 456->520, docs/rfc 336->364, and ADR-0063/0064/0065 + RFC-024/025/026 now present where before they had NO nodes at all; (b) a hand-written GUARDED .git/hooks/post-commit — deliberately NOT `graphify hook install`, which on this machine would write through the global post-commit symlink into the shared `_forward` dispatcher and inject a rebuild into every hook type in every repo (sandbox-proven); the guard compares nodes AND links and auto-restores on regression, and was OBSERVED firing; (c) CLAUDE.local.md gained a "Rebuilding the graph — code is safe, docs are NOT" section; (d) ~/.claude/bin/graphify-staleness.sh wired as a SessionStart hook in .claude/settings.local.json, tested 4 ways incl. a can-fail case that reproduces the exact 51-commit condition. KNOWN STEADY STATE, NOT A DEFECT: the post-commit hook PROTECTS but does not REFRESH — every code rebuild trips the edge guard, so it blocks every time and the code layer WILL drift from HEAD again. The staleness reporter is the compensating control; acting on it is a human decision. A "semantic sidecar re-merge" was designed to fix this and then KILLED by measurement — it would have hit the same dedup and shed the same edges, so it was 40-80 lines that would have shipped, looked reasonable and silently not worked. Full local roadmap + the unfiled upstream defect report + RECHECK.sh live in ~/.claude/notes/graphify/ (NOT in this repo, NOT in the session scratchpad which is ephemeral). NEXT SESSION: nothing here is queued or owed. The roadmap's P10 Current action remains discharged, so a "what's next?" urge is still a re-ratification moment with the operator, not a poll of the issue list — exactly as the 2026-07-19 handoff said. See gotchas 1a9911bd0585 + 808bcc45f6cc and decision 35c909622ec8.

Why: Continuity: the 2026-07-19 handoff is otherwise still the newest, and it describes a state where nothing had moved — a future session reading only that would have no idea the Understand layer was rebuilt, that a git hook now exists in this working tree, or that `graphify hook install` is unsafe here. The hook and the staleness reporter are machine-local and gitignored, so this brain entry is the ONLY record of them that travels with the repo. Recording the killed sidecar explicitly so it reads as a decision backed by measurement rather than an omission someone should "finish".
